candidates.name: triangulation_ignition_field
candidates.human_effect: Probability that co-present strangers convert silent proximity into a triggered exchange because a salient shared anchor sits ON the high-integration desire line, giving each a permissible reason to speak.
candidates.effect_sign: Raises triggered conversation / chance encounter for mobile strangers and loose acquaintances; ~0 for solo focus; irrelevant to a formed private huddle.
candidates.interaction_form: Threshold-gated spatial product: anchor_salience x integration/path-overlap, gated to ~0 unless the anchor's plan location falls within a proximity band of the max-integration desire-line ridge. The signal is strictly the CO-LOCATION, not either term.
candidates.computation: Locate anchor centroids + salience s; sample integration I along a d-metre buffer of the desire-line ridge; gate g decaying with normal-distance offset; field = s*I*g per region. ABSTAINS where plan inference UNKNOWN or no anchor detected.
literature: Whyte 1980: triangulation requires the prop to lie where people already cross
Hillier et al. 1993: natural movement — integration predicts pedestrian flow
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Fountain on the diagonal cross-path between two entrances (ignites talk) vs identical fountain in a planted dead alcove off all circulation (admired, no talk).
candidates.rationale: Cleanest expression of David's core social lever; interaction is genuinely co-location (average fails); B-tier computable now; strongest region-A-vs-B value. AMBER preserved (rides inferred plan).

candidates.name: triangulated_conversation_permission
candidates.human_effect: Where a shared focal anchor on the desire line both draws strangers AND the spot is acoustically intelligible enough for the triggered exchange to actually proceed rather than abort.
candidates.effect_sign: Raises probability triggered conversation matures; low = anchor becomes a look-but-don't-talk feature. For unacquainted people crossing a shared space.
candidates.interaction_form: Triangulation pull (salience x integration/path-overlap) multiplied by an acoustic-feasibility gate (local STI > ~0.5). Pull without hearability = 0; hearability without pull = 0.
candidates.computation: pull = salience * integration at anchor cell; STI_local = f(absorption, enclosure); value = pull * gate(STI_local>0.5). ABSTAINS without Tier-B integration.
literature: Whyte 1980: triangulation
Hongisto 2005: adequate mutual STI needed to sustain conversation
Hillier et al. 1993
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Anchor at a path crossing amid absorptive surfaces (encounters mature) vs same anchor in a hard echoing lobby (people gather, cannot converse).
candidates.rationale: Genuinely extends triangulation with an acoustic hearability interaction (a distinct compound, not double-count); needs acoustic proxy so AMBER; strong A-vs-B.

candidates.name: conversation_initiation_propensity
candidates.human_effect: Probability mere co-presence at a seat cluster converts into a triggered spoken exchange, driven by sociopetal (facing-in) orientation at conversational distance with low barriers.
candidates.effect_sign: Raises triggered conversation / perceived friendliness for lounge/collaboration; worsens outcomes for solitary focus workers (unwanted interruption).
candidates.interaction_form: Sociopetal gate x Hall-distance bump (peak in social band ~1.2-3.6 m) x (1-barrier_opacity) x inverted-U on intelligibility. Sociofugal orientation vetoes the product regardless of the other terms.
candidates.computation: Estimate seat facing vectors, pairwise facing-in fraction; bin centroid distances to Hall zones; barrier opacity along sightline; inverted-U on C3. ABSTAINS without recoverable seat orientation.
literature: Osmond 1957: sociopetal vs sociofugal
Sommer & Ross 1958: facing-in ~doubled conversation
Hall 1966; Mehrabian & Diamond 1971
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Sociopetal chairs around a low table at social distance (high) vs parallel outward rows at the same footfall (initiation collapses).
candidates.rationale: The sociofugal veto is a real non-additive interaction and central to David's ask; ABSTAINS without seat map so AMBER; high A-vs-B value.

candidates.name: huddle_shelter_viability
candidates.human_effect: Viability of a location for a formed pair/small group to hold a sustained or confidential exchange unseen and unheard by others; the deliberate inverse of the waiting/interaction seat.
candidates.effect_sign: Raises support for sustained huddle / confidential exchange; the SAME features worsen see-and-be-found suitability. Sign flips with occupant intent.
candidates.interaction_form: Product of seclusion complements: refuge x (1 - STI_to_others) x (1 - integration/exposure) x absorption. Any single high term (central location, acoustic leak) drives it toward zero; masking cross-check when visual closeness reads intimate but shelter is low.
candidates.computation: R=refuge; Priv=1-STI_to_others; Seg=1-normalized C1; value=R*Priv*Seg*absorption. ABSTAINS on the acoustic term without a material/absorption table or declared 3D model (SCORED-partial geometry vs UNKNOWN privacy).
literature: Sailer & McCulloh 2012: segregated locations host longer uninterrupted interaction
Osmond 1957 / Sommer 1969; Appleton 1975; ISO 3382-3
Alexander et al. 1977: alcoves / intimacy gradient
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Recessed backed absorptive alcove off the route (viable) vs open central node / glass-partition seat on a desire line (fails, masking flag).
candidates.rationale: The complementary optimum to triangulation; product-of-complements is a true compound; the six seats converged on it. Acoustic term abstains → AMBER. Strong A-vs-B.

candidates.name: reverberant_affect_masking_flag
candidates.human_effect: Cardinal masking trap: warm/wood/fluent materials lift rated pleasantness while a hard reflective envelope keeps RT60 high, so occupants report a lovely space yet pay a hidden listening-effort tax that cascades to fatigue and rougher interaction.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising it WORSENS the true outcome while improving the reported one; high flag = perception masking a physiological load. Worst for long-dwell and effortful listeners.
candidates.interaction_form: Conjunctive masking detector: affect_good (warm/wood/fluency) x reverb_bad (hard+specular, low-absorption), fires only when BOTH exceed threshold. Product of a positive perceptual signal with a negative physical one is the masking signature.
candidates.computation: affect=f(warm/cool, wood, fluency); reverb=(hard_coverage+specularity)*(1-absorption); flag=affect*reverb if both>0.5 else 0.
literature: Peelle 2018: listening effort is a measurable load even at maintained comprehension
Pichora-Fuller et al. 2016 (FUEL); Reber, Winkielman & Schwarz 1998/2004
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Wood-clad cafe with glass walls + hard floor (high flag) vs same palette with acoustic ceiling + textiles (flag ~0).
candidates.rationale: Textbook subjective-vs-physical divergence; Tier-A computable; strong evidence; the exemplary masking compound. AMBER (proxy RT60).

candidates.name: transit_vs_dwell_classifier
candidates.human_effect: Classifies a patch on the movement-vs-staying axis, deciding whether interaction there is fleeting (spine/chance encounter) or sustained (node/huddle/dwell).
candidates.effect_sign: A discriminator, not a valence: raising the ratio shifts toward fleeting co-presence and away from sustained interaction; mis-placing dwell furniture on a high-ratio spine worsens both flow and lingering.
candidates.social_form: chance encounter / fleeting co-presence (high) vs sustained huddle / dwell (low)
candidates.interaction_form: Ratio of movement potential to dwell potential: R=(I*K*p)/(refuge*enclosure+eps). The same integration means 'spine' when refuge/enclosure are low and 'dwell node' when high — the meaning is relational, not a level.
candidates.computation: move=I*K*p; stay=ref*E; R=move/(stay+0.05); tri-state label by thresholds. ABSTAINS if inferred connectivity low-confidence.
literature: Hillier 1996: through-movement (choice) vs to-movement (integration)
Gehl 2010: movement vs staying; Turner 2001; Whyte 1980
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Integrated corridor crossing with no refuge (spine, brief encounters) vs enclosed alcove one step off it (dwell node, huddles).
candidates.rationale: Genuine ratio compound (average would rate both alike); highly useful for optimiser placement; B-tier. AMBER.

candidates.name: focus_zone_viability
candidates.human_effect: Whether a patch can host cognitively protected activity (deep solo work, confidential one-to-one) or only pass-through; focus is a conjunction that fails on its single worst breach.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising it IMPROVES sustained concentration/restoration; a single acoustic, visual, or traffic leak destroys it regardless of the other three.
candidates.interaction_form: Weakest-link (min-gated) conjunction of {privacy, distraction distance, refuge, low-traffic, low compound-load}; the minimum component dominates, so one breach collapses the score.
candidates.computation: m=min(q1..q4); g=geometric mean; score=m^0.5*g^0.5, optionally times (1-total_load). ABSTAINS on privacy without plan/STI.
literature: Hongisto 2005: cost driven by worst intelligibility exposure
Banbury & Berry 2005; Appleton 1975; Kim & de Dear 2013
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Perimeter desk, protected back, acoustic shadow (viable) vs central cross-path desk with one nearby talk-hotspot (privacy min drags score to ~0.25).
candidates.rationale: Liebig-minimum is a real non-additive interaction; merges the multisensory and POE focus proposals; AMBER (inferred plan/STI).

candidates.name: approach_propensity_field
candidates.human_effect: Felt pull to physically enter/advance into a region — the neuroaesthetic precondition to any social interaction, since none happens where no one goes.
candidates.effect_sign: Raises entry likelihood and dwell for a newcomer at a boundary; does not help someone seeking retreat (see hominess/huddle fields).
candidates.interaction_form: Conjunctive multiplicative gate: curvature x openness(1-enclosure) x coherence(1-clutter), warmth as bounded modulator, with a sharp-angularity veto. Near-zero on any factor collapses the field.
candidates.computation: field=(c*openness*coherence)*(1+0.5*warm)*angular_veto, clamped [0,1]. ABSTAIN if plane/contour segmentation below threshold.
literature: Vartanian et al. 2013 (PNAS): curved interiors drive 'enter' decisions
Vartanian et al. 2015; Bar & Neta 2006; Coburn et al. 2020
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Curved, open, uncluttered reception volume (visitors advance/pause) vs rectilinear low-soffit corridor mouth with busy signage (pass through, no nucleation).
candidates.rationale: Conjunctive gate is genuinely more than parts; Tier-A computable; solid neuroaesthetics evidence. AMBER (proxy-grade approach link).

candidates.name: window_prospect_glare_antagonism
candidates.human_effect: Whether a prospect/window seat is actually dwelt in (restoration, see-and-be-found) or covertly avoided because glare is a co-located cost that vetoes the view/daylight resource.
candidates.effect_sign: Prospect+daylight improve dwell/restoration; glare worsens and can fully veto, so identical view depth yields opposite occupancy. Also masks perceived desirability against comfort.
candidates.interaction_form: Antagonism/veto: resource=(prospect x daylight) multiplied by (1 - glare_gate); high view + high glare -> near-zero dwell, whereas an average would report 'moderate'.
candidates.computation: resource=sqrt(prospect*daylight); glare_gate=sigmoid past discomfort threshold, scaled by glass*(1-shade_cue); value=resource*(1-glare_gate).
literature: Appleton 1975; Jakubiec & Reinhart 2012 (DGP); Osterhaus 2005; Boubekri et al. 1991
candidates.region_A_vs_B: North-shaded window seat (dwelt-in perch) vs west-facing glary window of identical view depth (empty at peak sun).
candidates.rationale: Sign-flipping antagonism is a true compound; merges the dwell and masking framings of the same physics; Tier-A. AMBER (proxy glare/daylight).

candidates.name: organized_complexity_dwell
candidates.human_effect: Effortless soft-fascination dwell from mid-band fractal fluency, but ONLY when richness is organized (low clutter, coherent); the same richness un-organized becomes clutter/visual discomfort that repels.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves restoration/dwell when coherence present; raising raw complexity without coherence WORSENS (overload/discomfort) — sign flips on the coherence/discomfort gate.
candidates.social_form: fleeting co-presence -> triggered conversation (lingering seeds contact)
candidates.interaction_form: Mid-fractal inverted-U x coherence gate (entropy/symmetry) x discomfort veto (1/f deviation). Complexity is conditioned on order; high fluency cannot rescue a high-discomfort scene (antagonism).
candidates.computation: band=exp(-((D-1.4)^2)/2s^2); coherence=(1-clutter)*(0.5+0.5*sym); disc_gate collapses as 1/f deviation rises; value=complexity*coherence*disc_gate; emit overload_flag.
literature: Kaplan & Kaplan 1989: complexity helps only with coherence
Taylor et al. 2011 / Hagerhall et al. 2004 (mid fractal ~1.3-1.5)
Reber et al. 2004; Fernandez & Wilkins 2008
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Rhythmic symmetrically-hung gallery wall (people slow, look, remark) vs equal-richness mismatched signage/ductwork (speed up, no talk).
candidates.rationale: Non-monotone coherence-gated fascination with discomfort veto is a real compound; all-GREEN primitives but proxy link -> AMBER; behavioural dwell ground truth.

candidates.name: sittable_prospect_ledge
candidates.human_effect: Dwell propensity of an edge seat and its capacity to hold a stationary person long enough to become a conversation seed or be found — high only when genuinely sittable AND commanding a view of the flow AND with a protected back.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves lingering/chance encounter/being-found for waiters and people-watchers; not for solitary retreat. Exposes code-masking where seat COUNT is met by seats that face a wall.
candidates.social_form: fleeting co-presence -> chance encounter / triggered conversation
candidates.interaction_form: Threshold-gated product sittability x prospect x back-refuge, each with a hard floor; a wall-facing bench has high seat-provision but zero prospect, so the product (not the mean) zeroes it.
candidates.computation: Normalize each; sigmoid gates at literature thresholds; value=product of gated terms per candidate seat/edge cell. ABSTAINS without a detectable seatable plane.
literature: Whyte 1980: sittable space dominates plaza use
Gehl 1987/2011: edge effect
Appleton 1975
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Low planter ledge, back to wall, facing entry stream (occupied, seeds talk) vs identical seat count as backless open-middle stools (empty).
candidates.rationale: Product-gate correctly zeroes wall-facing seats; strong Whyte/Gehl base; needs seat-plane detection so AMBER.

candidates.name: sun_seat_amenity_colocation
candidates.human_effect: Formation of a self-reinforcing staying cluster where a warm sun patch, a sittable surface, and a food/amenity anchor coincide within a few metres — the classic Whyte plaza-use engine.
candidates.effect_sign: Context-dependent sign: in cool conditions sun is a RESOURCE and co-location improves lingering/talk; in heat sun becomes a COST and the same co-location repels use (antagonism/timing flip).
candidates.social_form: triggered conversation / sustained huddle forming from a staying cluster
candidates.interaction_form: Spatial co-occurrence product within a radius, sign-modulated by a thermal gate: sittability x amenity_salience x sun_patch_overlap x sign(thermal_comfort). Two of three score low.
candidates.computation: Overlap area {sun ∩ sittable ∩ within r of amenity}; multiply; apply thermal sign-gate to [-1,+1]. Sun-patch from a single photo is heuristic -> ABSTAIN; GREEN-adjacent only with declared 3D/solar model.
literature: Whyte 1980: sun+food+seating strongest use correlates
Nikolopoulou & Lykoudis 2006; Gehl 1987/2011
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Cool-day sunlit ledge 2 m from a coffee cart (dense cluster) vs shaded seats 40 m from amenity (empty); flips in a July heatwave.
candidates.rationale: Real three-way co-occurrence with a genuine sign flip, but needs sun-patch geometry (single photo heuristic) and thermal input — low feasibility caps it. AMBER.

candidates.name: self_congestion_conversation_pinch
candidates.human_effect: The counter-intuitive Whyte hotspot where the highest share of people-in-conversation occurs — the busiest crossing/entry pinch where high through-movement plus a mild narrowing plus mutual visibility make people stop and talk mid-stream.
candidates.effect_sign: Inverted-U: raising movement-overlap and pinch improves stop-and-talk up to a crowding knee, then worsens it as perceived crowding exceeds comfort and people flee.
candidates.social_form: chance encounter / triggered conversation embedded in the moving stream
candidates.interaction_form: Threshold-gated non-monotone composition: path_overlap x pinch_narrowing x visibility, multiplied by an inverted-U crowding penalty that rises then falls at the comfort knee. Neither raw flow nor raw pinch alone predicts the peak.
candidates.computation: base=F*P*V; m=crowd*(1-crowd)/0.25 (peaks at moderate crowding); value=base*m. Rides inferred plan -> AMBER; ABSTAINS if movement grid UNKNOWN.
literature: Whyte 1980: self-congestion — most conversation in the center of pedestrian flow
Hillier et al. 1993; Gehl 1987/2011
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Doorway/corridor-merge pinch (people plant and talk mid-stream) vs wide even mid-corridor of equal traffic (pure pass-through).
candidates.rationale: Non-monotone crowding knee is a strong, well-evidenced interaction unique in the set; B-tier computable; high A-vs-B value.

candidates.name: triangulation_vs_decoration_ratio
candidates.human_effect: Masking-exposer separating an anchor that actually triangulates (salient AND on the movement network) from mere decoration (salient but off all desire lines) that inflates richness/awe scores while producing zero social contact.
candidates.effect_sign: High ratio means visual investment converts into conversation (improves contact); low ratio means the investment worsens the code's honesty by making a socially inert space read as rich. Whole-space diagnostic.
candidates.interaction_form: Ratio of integration-weighted salience to total salience: R=Σ(s_i*I_i)/Σ(s_i). Not an average — it asks what FRACTION of the attractor mass lands on the movement network; an off-path sculpture drives the numerator to 0.
candidates.computation: Detect anchors; R over all anchors; flag masking when C24/richness high but R low. AMBER (needs plan inference); ABSTAINS if anchor localisation UNKNOWN.
literature: Whyte 1980: triangulation works only on the path
Hillier & Hanson 1984: social yield depends on configurational position
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Modest screen/water feature on the main cross-axis (low awe, high ratio, much talk) vs spectacular atrium sculpture in a non-circulated void (high awe, ratio ~0).
candidates.rationale: Ratio catches the decoration-as-social-generosity mask that no single primitive sees; directly guards the awe/richness metrics; B-tier.

candidates.name: solitary_retreat_affordance
candidates.human_effect: Where a lone person can settle and reasonably expect NOT to be drawn into interaction or watched — the see-without-being-engaged seat, the deliberate inverse of conversation-initiation.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves restoration/focus for the solitary occupant (lower vigilance, defended space); worsens commons vitality if a whole region scores high.
candidates.interaction_form: Conditional min-gate: min(sociofugal, refuge_backing) x (1-path_overlap) x (1-STI_to_others). A plush high-refuge seat on a busy desire line is not a retreat — failing any one drags the product down.
candidates.computation: sociofugal=1-sociopetal; refuge behind seat; path_overlap at cell; STI leakage over surrounding planes. ABSTAINS without seat orientation.
literature: Sommer 1966: solo readers chose back-to-wall/corner seats
Appleton 1975; Hall 1966
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Sociopetal cluster at social distance (low) vs sociofugal back-to-wall single seat off the main path (high) — scores oppositely to C03 by construction.
candidates.rationale: Distinct interaction (min-gate) and human effect (retreat) from the huddle field; sharpens A-vs-B; needs seat orientation -> AMBER.

candidates.name: proxemic_intrusion_stress
candidates.human_effect: Stress/discomfort from forced personal-space intrusion — strangers pushed face-to-face inside intimate distance with no barrier and no escape — and its suppression of interaction; encodes the agency buffer.
candidates.effect_sign: Worsens stress/arousal/avoidance for the intruded occupant and suppresses interaction (people vacate/disengage). A barrier or occupant control lowers it for identical spacing.
candidates.interaction_form: Threshold-gated conditional: step(distance<~0.45 m) x facing_in x (1-barrier) x (1-control). Fires only below the intimate threshold; barrier OR control attenuate multiplicatively, so identical spacing scores very differently.
candidates.computation: Flag pairs under intimate threshold; weight by facing-in; multiply by (1-barrier)*(1-control). ABSTAINS without seat orientation; control term abstains unless declared.
literature: Evans & Wener 2007: close proximity raised crowding and cortisol
Hall 1966; Sommer 1969; Evans & Stecker 2004 (agency buffer)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Facing-in stranger pair crammed at intimate distance, no barrier/control (high stress) vs same spacing back-to-back with partition or adjustable seat (low).
candidates.rationale: Agency-buffer interaction (control changes the response to identical stimulus) is genuinely compound and well-evidenced; needs seat orientation + control input -> AMBER.

candidates.name: co_awareness_integration
candidates.human_effect: Whether being configurationally central actually translates into mutual awareness of other occupants — the precondition for a place feeling alive and for someone joining a group. Integration without sightlines to occupied space yields central-but-blind spots.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves co-presence-to-interaction conversion and perceived vitality/safety for weak-tie seekers; very high values worsen focus for deep solo work — sign flips with task.
candidates.social_form: triggered conversation / sustained-huddle joining / chance encounter
candidates.interaction_form: Product (not average): centrality (C1) x realized visibility of occupiable space (isovist from prospect). High C1 with low prospect (central but enclosed pocket) reads central on any single primitive but scores low here.
candidates.computation: I=normalized C1; V=isovist area weighted toward directions containing occupiable planes; co_awareness=I*V, minus a small pure-ceiling/floor term.
literature: Backhouse & Drew 1992: interaction tracks mutual visibility, not proximity
Rashid et al. 2006; Benedikt 1979 (isovist)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Integrated open-floor desk with long sightlines to the team (high) vs equally integrated desk behind a partition on the main route (passed by many, sees no one).
candidates.rationale: Clean product exposing the central-but-blind mask; only two primitives but the interaction is the whole signal; strong evidence and A-vs-B.

candidates.name: waiting_position_desirability
candidates.human_effect: Where a lone person will choose to wait/linger so as to both survey the space and be findable while keeping a protected back — the see-and-be-found position.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves perceived safety/comfort of solitary waiting AND keeps the waiter available for chance encounter; pushing integration too high worsens it (over-exposure) — an inverted-U.
candidates.social_form: solitary retreat that stays open to chance encounter
candidates.interaction_form: Product with an inverted-U (Gaussian, mu~0.6) weighting on integration: prospect x back-refuge x gauss(C1). Both very low and very high integration lower the score — findable but not exposed.
candidates.computation: Pr=forward isovist; Bk=back-occlusion/wall-adjacency; Mi=exp(-((C1-mu)^2)/2sigma^2); value=Pr*Bk*Mi per candidate cell.
literature: Appleton 1975; Hillier & Hanson 1984 (being-found needs moderate integration); Gehl 1987
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Bench against a wall facing the entrance on a moderately integrated line (findable, comfortable) vs open-middle stool with back to circulation and extreme through-movement (avoided/unsafe).
candidates.rationale: Inverted-U on integration is a distinct interaction from the plain product-gate seats; directly models David's lone-waiter form; AMBER.

candidates.name: collaboration_bump_field
candidates.human_effect: Where colleagues' routine paths to shared destinations overlap AND they are within communication range — Kabo's bump space — which, not raw proximity, predicts new weak-tie formation and unplanned work-relevant exchange.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves unplanned collaboration / weak-tie formation for distributed teams; can mildly worsen concentration for adjacent focus workers. Task-dependent.
candidates.interaction_form: Super-additive product: path_overlap x within-range(Allen curve) x amenity_pull. Overlap without range = anonymous passing; proximity without shared paths = silent neighbours; only the conjunction generates repeated range-close crossings.
candidates.computation: O=route-overlap density; Rng=distance decay per Allen curve (steep <8-15 m); Pull=amenity gravity; bump=O*Rng*Pull. ABSTAINS on C5 without seat/team map.
literature: Kabo et al. 2014: overlapping bump spaces predict new collaborations beyond proximity
Allen 1977 (Allen curve); Sailer & McCulloh 2012
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Shared corridor where two teams' routes to a common coffee point overlap within ~10 m (weak ties) vs equal-draw coffee point reached by non-overlapping single-team approaches (few new ties).
candidates.rationale: Path-overlap x range x amenity is a strong, well-evidenced super-additive compound; needs team map (abstains) -> AMBER.

candidates.name: group_audibility_reserve
candidates.human_effect: Whether a co-located group can all mutually hear one another well enough to sustain a multi-party huddle rather than a fractured conversation where the far side drops out.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves sustained-huddle formation and turn-taking for a collaborating group; the opposite optimum to confidential viability on outward reach. Task: 3-6 people problem-solving aloud.
candidates.interaction_form: Inverted-U (RT60 sweet-spot ~0.4-0.6 s from hard-surface vs absorption) x enclosure x 1/(mean within-cluster spacing). Value is reverberation-band x compactness x shielding, never their average.
candidates.computation: RT60_proxy=g(hard_coverage,1-absorption); w=triangular peaking 0.5 s, 0 outside [0.2,0.9]; value=w*enclosure*clamp(1.5/d). ABSTAINS without Tier-B cluster; RT60 is material-proxy.
literature: Bradley (classroom acoustics): mid RT + early reflections maximise multi-listener intelligibility
ANSI S12.60; Bronkhorst 2000 (cocktail-party)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Small enclosed mid-absorption room, tight seating (huddle sustains) vs table in a large hard-surfaced atrium (far side drops out).
candidates.rationale: RT60 inverted-U x compactness is a genuine non-additive interaction distinct from privacy fields; proxy RT60 -> AMBER.

candidates.name: speech_privacy_gradient
candidates.human_effect: The spatial decay steepness of intelligibility (ISO 3382-3 D2,S made a field): a steep gradient makes a space simultaneously good for local confidentiality AND concentration because conversations do not smear into neighbouring zones.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising it improves concentration and local privacy; lowering it triggers the acoustics->listening-effort->fatigue->rough-interaction cascade. Exposes the mask where a room feels quiet (low level) yet speech carries (shallow gradient).
candidates.interaction_form: Ratio/gradient: STI_within divided by STI_at-distraction-distance — a quotient of the same construct at two radii capturing decay steepness; an average of the two STIs would erase exactly the gradient wanted.
candidates.computation: STI_in from local absorption+enclosure; STI_out at ISO 3382-3 rD from the plan; gradient=STI_in/(STI_out+eps), normalized. ABSTAINS without Tier-B; proxy STI.
literature: Haapakangas/Hongisto ~2017; Sorqvist 2010 (irrelevant speech)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Absorptive partly-enclosed workpoint (neighbour's call unintelligible two seats away) vs open reverberant bench (every conversation carries despite low dBA).
candidates.rationale: Ratio-of-same-construct-at-two-radii is a real compound and exposes the low-level/shallow-gradient mask; borderline overlap with C8 distraction distance (noted); AMBER.

candidates.name: cocktail_party_suppression
candidates.human_effect: Babble build-up that collapses the speech-to-noise ratio so a would-be conversation cannot get started — the space forces shouting, a fleeting shouted exchange, or retreat.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising it WORSENS conversation initiation, pushing the likely form toward fleeting co-presence/retreat and away from triggered/sustained talk.
candidates.interaction_form: Super-additive with nonlinear occupancy: occupancy_density^2 x (1-absorption) x (1-enclosure). Competing-talker count matters far more when the envelope neither absorbs nor shields — babble accumulates multiplicatively, occupancy squared.
candidates.computation: occ=normalized talker density; suppression=clamp(occ^2*(1-absorption)*(1-enclosure),0,1). ABSTAINS without Tier-B occupancy.
literature: Cherry 1953 (cocktail-party); Bronkhorst 2000; Hongisto 2005
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Absorptive partly-enclosed nook at a given occupancy (conversation starts at normal voice) vs open hard hall at the SAME occupancy (only shouted fleeting exchanges).
candidates.rationale: Occupancy-squared multiplicative babble is a genuine super-additive interaction; needs occupancy grid -> AMBER.

candidates.name: felt_safety_field
candidates.human_effect: The below-awareness felt-safety FLOOR that gates whether a person will approach, stop, and stay in a region at all — the precondition for any lingering or social contact.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves safety/approach/lingering for any occupant; a low value suppresses dwell and drives avoidance regardless of amenity.
candidates.social_form: solitary retreat / fleeting co-presence (enabling floor beneath every form)
candidates.interaction_form: Threshold-gated MINIMUM (Liebig weakest-link): FSF=min(prospect, refuge_effective, escape, lighting_floor). A single deficient factor (unlit corner, blocked exit) collapses the field even if the other three are excellent.
candidates.computation: R_eff = enclosure as inverted-U (entrapment penalty at full enclosure); E from C1/decision-point preview; L=min local luminance in lower periphery; FSF=min(P,R_eff,E,L). ABSTAINS for E without inferred plan.
literature: Fisher & Nasar 1992 (concealment+prospect+escape combine)
Blobaum & Hunecke 2005; Boomsma & Steg 2014; Appleton 1975
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Glazed lobby edge with a lit backed alcove and visible exit (high) vs bright open center whose one recessed corner is unlit and exit occluded (FSF collapses on the min).
candidates.rationale: Weakest-link min is the correct non-additive form and well-evidenced (fear cues combine, not add); enabling floor for all social forms; AMBER (escape needs plan).

candidates.name: lingering_affordance
candidates.human_effect: Predicted dwell propensity — whether a person converts passing-through into staying. Dwell is the rate-limiting step for triggered conversation and chance encounter.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves triggered conversation/chance encounter by extending dwell; worsens throughput where flow is the goal (fit-dependent).
candidates.social_form: fleeting co-presence -> triggered conversation; chance encounter
candidates.interaction_form: Product gated by a crowding threshold: FSF x seat_refuge x prospect x (1-crowd_penalty), where the penalty engages only past the comfort knee — threshold-conditional, not linear subtraction.
candidates.computation: LA=FSF*seat_refuge*prospect*(C12<knee?1:(1-(C12-knee)/(1-knee))). ABSTAINS if C11/C12 unavailable.
literature: Whyte 1980 (dwell drives social life); Gehl 2011 (edge effect); Marcus & Francis 1997
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Backed perimeter bench facing the concourse (high dwell, high initiation) vs isolated island seat with no back and full exposure (brief rest, rarely engage).
candidates.rationale: Crowding-knee gate on a safety-conditioned product is genuinely compound; builds on C23 but adds a distinct threshold interaction; AMBER.

candidates.name: prospect_refuge_polarity
candidates.human_effect: One field, two opposite readouts: the lone waiter (prospect+protected back, see-and-be-found) and the formed huddle (refuge+low exposure+low speech intelligibility to others) have OPPOSITE prospect-refuge optima; scores per region which of the two it serves.
candidates.effect_sign: A signed suitability contrast: positive pole improves solitary waiting/being-found and worsens confidential huddling; the negative pole reverses it.
candidates.social_form: solitary retreat / waiting-to-be-found (positive) vs sustained huddle / confidential exchange (negative)
candidates.interaction_form: Signed DIFFERENCE of two threshold-products (antagonism): WaiterScore(prospect*findability*back) - HuddleScore(refuge*(1-exposure)*(1-STI_to_others)). The value lives entirely in the contrast; an average would cancel the signal.
candidates.computation: Normalize inputs; compute both scores; Polarity=Waiter-Huddle in [-1,1]; report both scalars and polarity per cell. ABSTAINS on STI/orientation without inferred plan.
literature: Appleton 1975 (prospect and refuge sometimes opposed)
Osmond 1957 / Sommer 1969; Gatersleben & Andrews 2013
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Near-entrance high-integration high-prospect spot (strongly positive) vs recessed low-STI enclosed alcove (strongly negative) — a good foyer contains cells at BOTH poles.
candidates.rationale: Directly operationalises David's waiter-vs-huddle antagonism as one signed field; the difference-of-products is a true compound; high A-vs-B value; AMBER.

candidates.name: safety_perception_masking_gap
candidates.human_effect: Exposes the cardinal safety mask: a fast bright-and-open cue makes a space FEEL safe while hidden concealment/entrapment geometry still loads threat physiology; flags regions where perceived safety overstates physical safety.
candidates.effect_sign: A large positive gap WORSENS actual safety while the surface read says otherwise — a hidden hazard; diagnostic that raising perceived-safety cues without fixing concealment is dangerous.
candidates.social_form: solitary retreat / chance encounter on a shared path
candidates.interaction_form: Signed disagreement between two composites (subjective->physical test): looks_safe(brightness, prospect, low clutter) - physical_safe(occlusivity, dark-corner luminance, entrapment). Its purpose is to be non-zero when a 'good' cue hides a 'bad'.
candidates.computation: looks_safe=mean(illuminance, prospect, 1-clutter); physical_safe=1-mean(occlusivity, entrapment, 1-corner luminance); GAP=looks_safe-physical_safe in [-1,1]. ABSTAINS where occlusivity/entrapment segmentation low.
literature: Fisher & Nasar 1992; Nasar, Fisher & Grannis 1993; Boomsma & Steg 2014
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Bright glazed lobby with a recessed unlit blind corner off the desire line (high positive gap) vs uniformly legible daylit corridor (gap ~0).
candidates.rationale: Cross-register divergence is the intended masking form (endorsed by the brief); each side is a composite but the value is their disagreement -> genuine compound; AMBER.

candidates.name: restorative_refuge_window
candidates.human_effect: Distinguishes a refuge that HEALS (a backed nook with an outward view) from a refuge that ALARMS (an enclosed low-prospect recess — entrapment); same 'refuge' label, opposite outcome, decided by whether prospect accompanies it.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves restoration/recovery when prospect accompanies refuge; worsens it (net stress, avoidance) when refuge is high but prospect is low — sign flips conditionally on prospect.
candidates.interaction_form: Conditional with a sign flip: above a prospect knee, refuge adds restorative value (prospect*refuge*(1+C19)); below it, refuge is penalized as entrapment (-(refuge-prospect)*occlusivity). Non-monotone in refuge.
candidates.computation: If prospect>=knee: RRW=prospect*refuge*(1+0.5*C19). If prospect<knee AND refuge>knee: RRW=-(refuge-prospect)*occlusivity. Output [-1,1]. ABSTAINS where prospect depth low-confidence.
literature: Gatersleben & Andrews 2013 (high-prospect/low-refuge more restorative; entrapment reduces it)
Appleton 1975; Herzog & Kropscott 2004
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Window-backed reading nook with outward view (high) vs interior windowless enclosed recess (negative — entrapment, avoided though quietest).
candidates.rationale: Encodes the exact Gatersleben-Andrews conditional with a genuine sign flip; Tier-A; AMBER (heuristic depth).

candidates.name: natural_surveillance_reassurance
candidates.human_effect: Felt safety in a POPULATED public setting: the reassurance of being watched-over by prosocial others while holding one's own protected position. An empty backed nook feels abandoned; a crowded exposed spot feels watched-at, not watched-over.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves felt safety and willingness for chance encounter when surveillance-by-others AND own-refuge co-occur; surveillance without refuge worsens it (exposure stress). Non-additive.
candidates.social_form: chance encounter / fleeting co-presence escalating to conversation
candidates.interaction_form: Co-occurrence product (both-or-neither): surveillance_by_others x own_refuge, each thresholded. High surveillance x low refuge = exposure; high refuge x zero surveillance = isolation; the reassuring cell is a spatial AND.
candidates.computation: surveillance=f(C1 overseeing vantages, occupancy-in-view, orientation of others); refuge=C11; NSR=product. REQUIRES occupancy/people-in-frame -> ABSTAINS without it (C1 alone gives only latent capacity).
literature: Jacobs 1961 (eyes on the street); Newman 1972 (defensible space); Loewen, Steel & Suedfeld 1993
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Overlooked-but-backed cafe edge along a busy concourse (high) vs isolated backstair landing with excellent refuge but no eyes on it (low, unsafe despite refuge).
candidates.rationale: Both-or-neither co-occurrence flags the refuge-alone-mistaken-for-safety failure; needs occupancy input (abstains) -> AMBER.

candidates.name: stranded_amenity_index
candidates.human_effect: Masking-exposure: an amenity can be visually attractive and fluent yet socially inert because it sits off the movement lattice; flags the gap between perceived invitingness and realisable encounter so a designer is not fooled by a pretty render.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising the index WORSENS realised social outcomes (high appeal, near-zero delivered encounters). A high value is bad — a warning signal for any commons programme relying on that amenity.
candidates.social_form: solitary retreat / dead amenity (intended interaction fails)
candidates.interaction_form: Ratio of attractiveness to movement: (salience x fluency) / (eps + integration x connectivity). Numerator = 'looks inviting', denominator = 'is on the flow'; the diagnostic value is their divergence, which no average expresses.
candidates.computation: index=(s*a)/(0.05+i*c), clipped [0,3]; flag cells >1.5 as stranded. High = attractive but predicted under-used.
literature: Whyte 1980 (attractive but off-line plazas sat empty); Gehl 2010; Reber, Schwarz & Winkielman 2004 (fluency inflates appeal)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Beautifully furnished lounge at a dead-corridor end (high index, empty) vs plain bench on the main spine (low index, constantly used).
candidates.rationale: Attractiveness/movement ratio catches the render-vs-use mask that designers fall for; B-tier computable; high value to the honesty of the social prediction.

candidates.name: restorative_social_nook_score
candidates.human_effect: A low-arousal restorative micro-setting where involuntary micro-breaks and unforced informal talk occur, as opposed to a high-arousal work grid — Kaplan soft fascination co-occurring with acoustic and material softness.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves restoration AND informal sociality for occupants on a break/in transit; for deep-focus solo work the same softness can reduce alerting, so sign flips by task.
candidates.social_form: triggered conversation (with sustained huddle as settled form)
base_measures: soft-material composite (specularity inverse, texture homogeneity, wood/upholstery coverage)
candidates.interaction_form: Threshold-gated geometric mean (spatial AND-gate): each of the four must clear a floor; the score is the product of floored terms, so any single missing ingredient (no greenery, hard surfaces, glare-only light) collapses it.
candidates.computation: Floor each at t=0.2 via relu; score=(veg'*soft'*absorb'*day')^(1/4). ABSTAIN if material segmentation UNKNOWN for the dominant seating plane.
literature: Coley, Kuo & Sullivan 1997 (greenery raised social use); Kuo & Sullivan 2001; Alvarsson et al. 2010; Kaplan 1995
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Planted corner with upholstered seating, textiles, glare-controlled daylight (micro-breaks + talk) vs open desk grid, hard floor, overhead-only light (fleeting co-presence, no exchange).
candidates.rationale: AND-gated geometric mean (a maxed-daylight bare grid scores ~0) is genuinely compound; needs material segmentation -> AMBER.

candidates.name: recovery_seat_index
candidates.human_effect: Marks a seat affording Ulrich stress recovery for a lone occupant: a nature-bearing outward view combined with a protected back — the see-and-be-found recovery position.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves restoration and lowers stress arousal for a solitary waiting/recovering occupant; does not help a formed huddle (which wants the inverse) — sign is occupant-state-specific.
candidates.social_form: solitary retreat, adjacent to chance encounter (stays findable)
candidates.interaction_form: Content-gated product: prospect x refuge x veg-in-view. A deep view of a parking lot (veg~0) or a nature view with an exposed back (refuge~0) both drive the product low — nature content and refuge must multiply.
candidates.computation: recovery=prospect*veg_in_view*refuge, all normalized [0,1]. ABSTAIN if neither Tier-B plan nor a clear seat plane is detected.
literature: Ulrich 1984 (window view sped recovery); Ulrich et al. 1991 (SRT); Appleton 1975; Dosen & Ostwald 2016
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Window-side wall-backed chair facing a planted courtyard (high) vs backless mid-floor bench facing a blank wall (low).
candidates.rationale: Content-gating (nature must be IN the view cone, not the whole frame) makes this more than a prospect+refuge average; strong Ulrich evidence; AMBER (veg rides segmentation).

candidates.name: soft_fascination_field
candidates.human_effect: Effortless involuntary attention (Kaplan soft fascination) from natural content at moderate, orderly complexity — restorative because it engages attention without depleting it; supports lingering that seeds chance social contact.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves directed-attention restoration and dwell when complexity is mid-band; raising complexity past the band (clutter) worsens it (overload) — an inverted-U.
candidates.social_form: chance encounter on a shared path (lingering raises co-presence dwell)
candidates.interaction_form: Band-pass gate x suppressor: veg_frac x bandpass(fractal ~1.3-1.5) x (1-clutter above threshold). The fractal term is a non-monotone inverted-U filter; clutter is a multiplicative suppressor — mid-band natural texture with LOW clutter required simultaneously.
candidates.computation: band=exp(-((D-1.4)^2)/2*0.09); supp=relu(1-relu(clutter-0.5)/0.5); score=veg_frac*band*supp.
literature: Kaplan & Kaplan 1989 / Kaplan 1995 (soft fascination); Hagerhall et al. 2004; Taylor 2006; Joye & van den Berg 2011 (contested)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Foliage-textured planted screen at mid fractal complexity beside a path (lingering + incidental encounters) vs over-cluttered graphic wall (pass-through).
candidates.rationale: Biophilic variant of the fractal-fascination interaction (vegetation-specific); overlaps C10 in mechanism but distinct nature construct; AMBER (veg rides segmentation); restoration link partly contested.

candidates.name: green_commons_gravity
candidates.human_effect: Greenery located AT a well-integrated, path-crossing commons node draws voluntary gathering and triggered conversation; the same greenery off the movement network stays unused.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves informal gathering/connectedness only when co-located with high integration; greenery on a dead-end has near-zero social lift — the benefit is entirely conditional on placement.
candidates.social_form: triggered conversation / chance encounter at a shared restorative anchor
base_measures: C1 visual integration / C23 commons gravity
candidates.interaction_form: Spatial co-occurrence product on a movement field: veg_frac x integration x seating_present. Integration near-0 off the path zeroes isolated greenery — the signal is the JOINT location of nature + integration + a reason to stop.
candidates.computation: gravity=veg_frac*integ*seat, all [0,1]. ABSTAIN without Tier-B plan or declared integration; integration rides inferred plan.
literature: Coley, Kuo & Sullivan 1997; Sullivan, Kuo & DePooter 2004; Whyte 1980 (triangulation); Hillier & Hanson 1984
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Planted seating cluster at the atrium's most integrated crossing (gathering, stranger talk) vs identical planter in a low-integration dead-end (near-zero social use).
candidates.rationale: Structurally a triangulation variant with greenery as anchor, but the vegetation->social-use evidence (Coley/Kuo) is distinct enough to retain; borderline-with-C01 noted; AMBER.

candidates.name: daylit_talk_pocket
candidates.human_effect: The documented synergy setting: glare-controlled daylight WITH acoustic absorption jointly cut visual strain and listening effort, yielding larger mood-and-conversation gains than either alone; uncontrolled glare inverts it.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves sustained conversation/mood when glare is controlled; worsens (visual strain, avoidance, no talk) once glare passes threshold — non-monotone in glazing via the glare gate.
candidates.social_form: sustained conversation / huddle in a comfortable daylit pocket
candidates.interaction_form: Super-additive product with an inverting gate: (daylight x absorption) x (1 - 2*relu(glare-g0)). Below threshold the terms multiply (synergy > either alone); above it the gate turns negative (antagonism). A sum/average cannot represent the sign flip.
candidates.computation: gate=1-2*relu(glare-0.4)/0.6 in [-1,1]; pocket=day*absorb*gate in [-1,1] (negative = glare-dominated, avoid).
literature: Boubekri et al. 2014 (daylight->mood/vitality); Beute & de Kort 2014; Sarampalis et al. 2009 (listening effort); Ulrich et al. 1991
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Soft-furnished corner with diffuse shaded daylight + absorptive panels (positive talk pocket) vs unshaded south glass wall with hard surfaces (negative — glare + echo suppress talk).
candidates.rationale: Encodes the brief's flagship synergy+antagonism with a genuine sign flip; Tier-A; AMBER (all three proxy-grade).

candidates.name: hominess_nook_index
candidates.human_effect: Felt hominess/belonging and willingness to settle, linger, and lower one's guard — the affective substrate for sustained and confidential exchange rather than transient contact.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves willingness to stay and hold sustained/confidential conversation for a small group or refuge-seeker; deliberate tension with approach (which wants openness) — a good foyer needs both in different regions.
candidates.interaction_form: Inverted-U on enclosure GATED by warmth and softness: warmth_softness x bell(enclosure, peak ~0.55). Too open reads institutional, too enclosed cramped; warmth alone in an open hall is not homey — the product is the signal.
candidates.computation: warmth_soft=0.5*warm+0.3*wood+0.2*homogeneity; bell(e)=exp(-((e-0.55)^2)/2*0.18^2); hominess=warmth_soft*bell(e). ABSTAIN if material confidence low (wood vs laminate).
literature: Coburn et al. 2020 (hominess as a distinct dimension); Chatterjee & Vartanian 2014; Kaplan & Kaplan 1989
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Carpeted wood-lined banquette alcove (settle and talk at length) vs open glass-walled atrium bench (pleasant but exposed, exchanges stay brief).
candidates.rationale: Inverted-U enclosure gated by warmth is a real non-additive interaction; Coburn evidence emerging; wood-vs-laminate ambiguity caps at AMBER.

candidates.name: angular_specular_avoidance
candidates.human_effect: Implicit threat/vigilance and avoidance — the aversive pole opposite approach. Sharp contours signal potential harm; hard specular surfaces amplify perceived hardness and glare-linked discomfort.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising it worsens approach and elevates baseline vigilance/stress for anyone, suppressing lingering and all higher social forms; danger is that such spaces read as 'premium' while loading physiology — a prestige mask.
candidates.social_form: fleeting co-presence only (traverse, do not settle)
candidates.interaction_form: Super-additive (product) threat co-occurrence: angularity x specularity, boosted by edge-sharpness and glare. Sharp AND glossy AND high-contrast is worse than the sum — a matte angular form or a glossy curved form is far less aversive.
candidates.computation: avoidance=(a*p)*(0.6+0.4*edge)*(1+0.4*glare), clamped [0,1].
literature: Bar & Neta 2006/2007 (sharp contours threatening, amygdala); Vartanian et al. 2013; Coburn et al. 2020 (specularity-threat inferential — uncertainty flag)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Sharp-cornered polished-stone-and-mirror lobby edge (impressive in photo, people keep moving) vs soft matte curved seating cluster (people settle and talk).
candidates.rationale: Threat co-occurrence is a genuine product and the negative pole of approach; specularity-threat link is inferential (proposer's own uncertainty flag) so evidence caps it; AMBER.

candidates.name: threshold_invitation_gradient
candidates.human_effect: Whether a boundary/decision-point actively INVITES advance or REPELS it, via the spatial gradient of approach-propensity across the threshold combined with how much of what lies beyond is previewed (mystery vs blankness).
candidates.effect_sign: A positive gradient with PARTIAL preview improves entry (mystery pull); full preview gives a weaker satisfied pull; a flat/negative gradient or high approach with zero preview (blank occlusion) worsens entry (people balk).
candidates.social_form: chance encounter / triggered conversation (inviting thresholds route people onto shared desire lines)
candidates.interaction_form: Spatial-gradient x preview interaction: delta_field (far minus near across threshold) modulated by an inverted-U on preview (mystery_pull peaks at partial occlusion). Neither the gradient nor the preview alone predicts invitation.
candidates.computation: invitation=delta_field * exp(-((preview-0.5)^2)/2*0.2^2), prospect as tiebreak, in [-1,1]. ABSTAIN if the onward region is not imaged/inferable.
literature: Kaplan & Kaplan 1989 (mystery a strong preference predictor); Vartanian et al. 2015; Appleton 1975
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Curved brightening opening revealing ~half a welcoming room (strongly positive) vs flat door into a fully-occluded blank vestibule, or full-glazed wall with nothing withheld (near-zero/negative).
candidates.rationale: Gradient-x-mystery is genuinely compound (and compounds over another compound); needs two region samples across a threshold so feasibility caps it; AMBER.

candidates.name: fluency_discomfort_mask
candidates.human_effect: A masking detector: a scene that reads as smooth/fluent/pleasant to conscious judgement while its spatial-frequency structure covertly loads the visual system (asthenopia, headache, gaze aversion) — perception says 'fine', physiology says 'cost'.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising the gap WORSENS hidden visual-stress load while subjective comfort stays flat or positive — the cardinal mask; it silently erodes dwell and mood downstream.
candidates.interaction_form: Conditional co-occurrence flag — the signal is the DISAGREEMENT: fires only when fluency is HIGH and spectral discomfort is HIGH simultaneously (AND-gated product); either alone is not a mask.
candidates.computation: mask_risk=fluency*spectral_deviation, evaluated only where both exceed their medians (else 0). High = looks fluent, physiologically costly -> route to ABSTAIN on dwell/comfort claims.
literature: Fernandez & Wilkins 2008 (uncomfortable images deviate from 1/f); Wilkins 1995; O'Hare & Hibbard 2011; Reber et al. 2004
candidates.rationale: AND-gated fluency-vs-discomfort divergence is a valid masking compound with strong visual-stress evidence; only two primitives and no social A-vs-B, so value to the social goal is lower; AMBER.

candidates.name: cozy_dim_circadian_deficit
candidates.human_effect: A warm, dim, 'cozy/restorative'-reading scene delivers a sub-threshold melanopic dose, so daytime alertness and circadian entrainment are undersupplied while the affect signal is strongly positive — pleasantness masks a biological-light deficit.
candidates.effect_sign: Timing-conditional: in the morning/daytime raising it worsens alertness/entrainment (deficit hidden by comfort); in the evening the same warmth is appropriate. Without a time-of-day input the sign ABSTAINS.
candidates.interaction_form: Product-of-antagonists across a nonlinear knee: coziness_affect x melanopic_deficit, where deficit is a threshold function (steep below the entrainment knee). Value is the co-occurrence of high hedonic warmth with sub-knee estimated melanopic level.
candidates.computation: deficit=clamp((knee-vEDI_proxy)/knee,0,1) with knee ~ daytime target; score=warmth*deficit. Requires time_of_day to resolve sign; ABSTAINS on sign without it and entirely if illuminance proxy UNKNOWN.
literature: Brown et al. 2022 (daytime melanopic EDI >=250 lux); Lucas et al. 2014; Chang et al. 2015 (evening light delays phase)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Warm lounge corner (restorative, under-doses morning light) vs daylight-adjacent seat (delivers melanopic dose); code should nudge to the latter for a morning task despite the former feeling nicer.
candidates.rationale: Nonlinear-knee product-of-antagonists with an honest timing abstention; melanopic-from-photo is heavily proxy and needs time input, so feasibility caps it; AMBER.

candidates.name: biophilic_air_quality_halo
candidates.human_effect: A 'green = healthy' halo: visible vegetation plus warm natural materials in an enclosed, likely under-ventilated volume generate a restoration/health affect that masks elevated CO2/VOC load — occupants feel the air is fresh because the room looks natural.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising it WORSENS the reliability of any 'this room is healthy' inference; the CO2 cognitive decrement is undetectable from the image and must be measured. Task hit: sustained cognitive work.
candidates.social_form: sustained huddle / behaviour-setting fit (people stay longer, raising CO2 they cannot perceive)
candidates.interaction_form: Product with an enclosure gate: nature_halo x stagnation_proxy. Nature contact alone (open, ventilated) scores low; the compound fires only where the restorative cue co-occurs with high enclosure (proxy for low fresh-air exchange).
candidates.computation: halo=normalized(C19+veg+wood); stagnation=enclosure with no visible operable aperture; score=halo*stagnation. Marks CO2 as extra_input; ABSTAINS on physiological magnitude until a CO2 reading is supplied.
literature: Allen et al. 2016 (COGfx: cognition falls as CO2 rises); Satish et al. 2012; Cummings & Waring 2020 (potted plants negligible CO2/VOC removal)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Planted enclosed nook (long stays raise CO2 unseen) vs open plant-light but ventilated zone.
candidates.rationale: Enclosure-gated halo is a genuine interaction and the Cummings-Waring finding kills the halo cleanly, but enclosure is a weak ventilation proxy and the physiological term abstains without CO2 -> low feasibility; AMBER.

candidates.name: visual_refuge_acoustic_leak_false_shelter
candidates.human_effect: A false-shelter trap for confidential exchange: a nook that is VISUALLY enclosed and sheltered but acoustically leaky (hard low-absorption surfaces, high STI to bystanders) — occupants feel private and speak freely while being overheard.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising it WORSENS confidential-exchange viability; the seat reads as safe to speak but carries uncorrected speech-transmission cost — a discount on any refuge/privacy scoring. Task hit: sensitive one-to-one.
candidates.interaction_form: Antagonistic ratio: visual_refuge / acoustic_privacy — rises when visual shelter is high AND acoustic privacy low. Two independent privacy channels pulled apart; a nook high on both, or low on both, scores near zero. The divergence IS the attribute.
candidates.computation: visual_refuge=norm(enclosure+C11); acoustic_leak=1-min(absorption, privacy_from_STI); score=visual_refuge*acoustic_leak. ABSTAINS if STI needs an occupancy map that is UNKNOWN.
literature: ISO 3382-3 (STI/privacy distance independent of visual enclosure); Bradley & Gover 2004; Passero & Zannin 2012
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Visually screened but hard-surfaced alcove (high false-shelter, invites talk it cannot protect) vs soft-furnished textile-lined corner (low — route confidential exchange here).
candidates.rationale: Two-privacy-channel divergence directly sharpens the confidential-exchange field David asked for; genuine ratio compound; needs STI/plan -> AMBER.

candidates.name: hue_heat_thermal_comfort_illusion
candidates.human_effect: A cross-modal thermal illusion: a warm-hued palette plus large solar-gaining glazing lifts perceived warmth/comfort, masking real thermal load — an over-warm sunlit room still looks cozy, or a cool room reads warm enough that discomfort is under-reported.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising it WORSENS detection of the real thermal cost; because the hue-heat literature is MIXED, the honest output is a divergence FLAG, not a temperature claim.
candidates.interaction_form: Product-of-antagonists: visual_warmth x solar_thermal_exposure. Visual warmth without glazing (stable interior) scores low; the compound fires where warm appearance co-occurs with an uncorrected thermal driver.
candidates.computation: score=visual_warmth*solar_exposure. Marks air-temp/RH as extra_input; ABSTAINS on sign/magnitude of thermal discomfort until a temperature reading is supplied.
literature: Fanger et al. 1977 (small hue-heat effect); Toftum et al. 2018 (small/inconsistent — hedge); Tsushima et al. 2020
candidates.rationale: Compound in form, but the underlying hue-heat interaction is by the proposer's own account small and inconsistent (contested link -> honest abstention), and the thermal magnitude abstains — lowest-value survivor, retained only as a flagged uncertainty; AMBER.

candidates.name: total_environmental_load_superadditive
candidates.human_effect: Cumulative sensory/physiological stress load and the collapse of a region's usability for demanding work; when two stressors co-occur the load exceeds their sum, so a region that looks only mildly bad on every primitive is actually unusable for focus.
candidates.effect_sign: Worsens sustained-attention performance, elevates arousal/fatigue, and demotes a region from focus-capable to transit-only; worst for demanding solitary cognitive work.
candidates.interaction_form: Threshold-gated super-addition: each stressor soft-thresholded above its own knee, then a pairwise PRODUCT (synergy) term added on top of the sum — the value comes from the product of co-active stressors, zero when only one is active.
candidates.computation: s_i=max(0,(x_i-k_i)/(1-k_i)); base=Σs_i; synergy=beta*(s_g*s_r+s_g*s_t+s_r*s_t), beta~0.6; load=clip(base+synergy,0,1).
literature: Hygge & Knez 2001 (noise/heat/light interact non-additively); Witterseh, Wyon & Clausen 2004; Schweiker et al. 2020
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Sunlit desk near untreated glazing (high glare + reverberation -> load ~0.8, transit-only) vs shaded absorptive alcove (~0.2, supports huddle/deep work), even though each single primitive is only 'moderately' bad in the former.
candidates.rationale: The explicit synergy term (not the sum) is the compound and is well-evidenced as non-additive; base for two other compounds; Tier-A; AMBER.

candidates.name: agency_buffered_load
candidates.human_effect: Discounts total environmental load wherever occupants have VISIBLE means to change their conditions (dimmer, operable blind, openable window, movable furniture); the same stressor produces a smaller stress response when controllable — the residual, agency-corrected load.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising visible control LOWERS effective load and stress for the same stimulus; strongest for dwelling occupants, negligible for pass-through.
candidates.interaction_form: Conditional multiplicative discount: buffered_load = total_load x (1 - lambda*agency). Agency changes the SLOPE of stimulus->stress, a true interaction, not an average of load and control.
candidates.computation: buffered_load=L*(1-lambda*a), lambda~0.4. ABSTAINS if neither C17 nor a controls detector/declared model is available — control presence is not reliably recoverable from a single photo.
literature: Glass & Singer 1972 (controllability cut noise aftereffects); Boerstra et al. 2016; Leaman & Bordass 1999 (perceived control a 'killer variable')
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Reverberant sunlit bench, no controls (full load ~0.8) vs identical physics with an operable blind and movable chair (~0.55, more usable).
candidates.rationale: Slope-changing agency buffer is genuinely compound and well-evidenced, but control presence abstains from a single photo -> feasibility caps it; AMBER.

candidates.name: thermal_acoustic_misattribution_index
candidates.human_effect: Flags regions where a thermally NEUTRAL space is likely reported 'too warm/stuffy' because acoustic stress inflates perceived warmth — a misattribution trap: the complaint points at HVAC but the driver is the soundscape.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising acoustic stress in a thermally neutral zone raises the perceived-warmth error and thermal dissatisfaction that HVAC tuning cannot fix; misleads facilities toward the wrong remedy.
candidates.interaction_form: Conditional gated shift: kappa x reverberation x neutral_band(warmth) — nonzero ONLY when physical warmth sits in the neutral band AND reverberation is high; the co-occurrence (product with a band gate), not an average.
candidates.computation: neutral_band(w)=bump peaking w in [0.4,0.6]; index=kappa*r*neutral_band(w), kappa~0.5; high = predicted thermal complaint that is really acoustic.
literature: Pellerin & Candas 2003/2004 (noise shifts thermal preference); Schweiker et al. 2020; Torresin et al. 2021
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Thermally neutral open plan with hard reverberant finishes (high index, will be called 'stuffy/warm') vs same air temperature with absorptive finishes (low index, accurate appraisal).
candidates.rationale: Band-gated cross-domain misattribution is a real interaction with emerging evidence; no social A-vs-B and needs a thermal-neutrality assumption from a photo -> mid value; AMBER.

candidates.name: load_gated_conversation_suppression
candidates.human_effect: Corrects geometry-only predictions of triggered conversation: a sociopetal, well-anchored spot on a desire line still will NOT spark talk under high compound environmental load — favourable geometry is necessary but not sufficient.
candidates.effect_sign: Raising compound load suppresses triggered conversation and chance encounters even where seating and a shared anchor would otherwise invite them.
candidates.interaction_form: Multiplicative gate: social_geometry x (1 - total_load). Environmental load MULTIPLIES down the geometric conversation signal — the prediction lives in the product; averaging load into geometry would hide fully-suppressed hotspots.
candidates.computation: sg=f(salience, integration, sociopetal if available); initiation=sg*(1-L). ABSTAINS on the sociopetal term without a seat/orientation input.
literature: Whyte 1980 (triangulation); Osmond 1957 / Sommer 1969; Glass & Singer 1972 & Cohen 1980 (stress reduces prosocial engagement)
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Sociopetal seats around a fountain on a high-integration path but under glare+reverberation (sg~0.8, L~0.7, initiation~0.24 — geometry-only overpredicts) vs identical seating in a shaded absorptive pocket (L~0.2, initiation~0.64).
candidates.rationale: Couples the social-geometry stack to environmental load — a genuinely corrective compound-over-compound that guards against over-optimistic conversation hotspots; AMBER.

candidates.name: designed_in_conflict_flag
candidates.human_effect: Neighbour concentration loss and stress imposed by a seating cluster that LOOKS collaborative but leaks speech into adjacent solitary-work space, while the collaboration itself self-degrades; the zone reads 'alive/buzzy' subjectively while loading the physiology of people it disrupts.
candidates.effect_sign: Worsens focus/restoration for occupants seated adjacent to the flagged patch and degrades the conversation's own confidentiality; a defect flag, not a resource.
candidates.social_form: collision of a triggered conversation/huddle with the solitary retreat it sits beside
candidates.interaction_form: Threshold-gated co-occurrence AND-gate: fires only when enclosure LOW AND absorption LOW AND STI-leak HIGH AND path-overlap HIGH in the same patch; value = product of the four breaches. Any single component below threshold zeroes it.
candidates.computation: openness=(1-E); acoustic_exposure=(1-A); flag=openness*acoustic_exposure*STI_leak*path_overlap if all four>0.6 else 0. ABSTAIN if plane segmentation low or STI not derivable.
literature: Kim & de Dear 2013 (loss of speech privacy the largest open-plan dissatisfier); Banbury & Berry 2005; Hongisto 2005; Osmond 1957
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Sociopetal cluster under an absorptive soffit in a low-traffic eddy (contained, ~0) vs identical inward seating on a reverberant hard transit spine (flag ~0.8) — same appearance, opposite scores.
candidates.rationale: Four-way AND-gated conflict detector catches the buzzy-vs-disruptive mask that measures the WRONG occupants' physiology; strong workplace evidence; AMBER.

candidates.name: belonging_anchorage
candidates.human_effect: Place attachment and territorial belonging of an occupant's home patch; predicts team cohesion, sustained presence, and identity-based well-being. Belonging needs a defensible, enclosed, personalizable, materially warm patch — not merely a seat.
candidates.effect_sign: Improves belonging/territoriality/connectedness (sustained presence, stronger ties); depriving it (hot-desking, clean-desk, cold open bench) worsens attachment.
candidates.social_form: sustained presence / team huddle grounded in a claimed territory
candidates.interaction_form: Enclosure-threshold-gated product with a personalization multiplier: belonging = territoriality x warmth x (1 + personalization), gated so that below a minimum enclosure the whole term collapses (no territory without a defensible edge). Personalization multiplies, not adds.
candidates.computation: gate=(E>=0.4)?1:E/0.4; belonging=gate*T*warmth*(1+pers). If personalization UNKNOWN -> ABSTAIN (report partial).
literature: Wells 2000 (personalization->well-being); Knight & Haslam 2010; Altman 1975 (territoriality); Sundstrom 1986
candidates.region_A_vs_B: Assigned team neighbourhood with enclosed backs, warm timber, personalized desks (strong anchorage) vs clean-desk hot-bench open plan, cold glass/metal, no personal artifacts (near-zero, or ABSTAIN if personalization unreadable).
candidates.rationale: Enclosure-gate + personalization multiplier is genuinely compound; personalization is an extra input the photo often lacks (honest abstain) so feasibility caps it; AMBER.

dropped: No proposal was dropped as non-compound: all 68 supply >=2 base measures combined by a genuine product, threshold-gate, ratio, inverted-U, sign-flip, or cross-register difference — none is a single measure or a bare average, and none merely restates a C1-C24 metric verbatim. The 30 near-duplicate writeups listed below were absorbed by merge (recorded in each survivor's merged_from), NOT dropped.

MERGED (triangulation core) into C01 triangulation_ignition_field: P1, P10, P13, P29, P30, P49 — all 'salient anchor co-located on the high-integration/path-crossing desire line converts co-presence to conversation'; same human effect + same co-occurrence-gated-product interaction. Third-term variants (prospect, enclosure, sociopetal, dwell) noted; best writeup = P1 (B-tier, cleanest desire-line gate).

MERGED (triangulation + acoustic hearability) into C02: P22, P65 — triangulation gated by a local-STI feasibility gate; retained as a distinct candidate because the acoustic gate is an added interaction, not a duplicate of C01.

MERGED (sociopetal conversation) into C03: P6, P34 — 'sociopetal seating raises triggered conversation'; sociofugal-veto gate x distance/intelligibility. P34 adds the inverted-U on intelligibility, folded in.

MERGED (huddle/confidential viability) into C04: P8, P14, P17, P33, P50, P68 — all 'enclosed + acoustically private + off-integration -> unseen/unheard exchange'; product of seclusion complements. P8's closeness-vs-shelter masking cross-check folded in.
MERGED (warm-look reverberant mask) into C05: P20, P37, P52 — identical conjunctive 'affect-good x acoustic-bad fires when both high' masking detector.

MERGED (movement-vs-dwell classifier) into C06: P11, P32, P66 — ratio of through-movement to dwell/refuge classifying spine vs node. P11's pause-affordance (seating+widening) folded into the dwell denominator.
MERGED (solo focus zone) into C07: P61, P64 — weakest-link/min conjunction of low-load + privacy + refuge + low-traffic for concentrative/confidential capacity.

MERGED (curved-contour approach) into C08: P41, P51 — curvature x openness x coherence with an angular veto driving approach. P44 (angular_specular_avoidance) was NOT merged: it is the distinct threat/avoidance pole with specularity+glare, retained as C36.

MERGED (view-seat glare antagonism) into C09: P48, P53 — prospect/daylight resource vetoed by co-located glare; P48's dwell framing and P53's perceived-desirability masking framing are the same underlying physics.

MERGED (fractal fluency dwell) into C10: P43, P46 — mid-fractal fluency gated by clutter/spectral-disorder -> dwell/fascination. P38 (soft_fascination_field) was NOT merged: it is the vegetation-gated biophilic variant with distinct Kaplan/biophilia evidence, retained as C32.

BORDERLINE-RETAINED, not dropped: C21 speech_privacy_gradient (P19) sits close to C8 distraction distance (both ISO 3382-3 spatial-decay constructs) but is a ratio of STI at two radii that exposes the low-level/shallow-gradient mask, so it adds interaction beyond the single C-metric — kept with a noted caveat and moderate score.

BORDERLINE-RETAINED, not dropped: C33 green_commons_gravity (P39) is structurally the triangulation interaction with greenery as the anchor (could have merged into C01), but the vegetation->social-use evidence base (Coley/Kuo/Sullivan) and dual restoration+social effect are distinct enough to retain separately; flagged for the panel.

LOWEST-VALUE RETAINED, not dropped: C42 hue_heat_thermal_comfort_illusion (P57) is compound in form but rests on a hue-heat link its own author calls 'small and inconsistent'; per honesty constraint 3 it is kept only as a flagged uncertainty/abstention (rank 4.0), not dropped, since weak construct validity is not a drop criterion — non-compound-ness or C1-C24 duplication is.

C07.is_it_really_compound: YES, genuinely. min(q1..q4) is a Liebig weakest-link gate: the minimum component dominates, so one acoustic/visual/traffic breach collapses the score regardless of the other three. That is a true non-additive interaction, not an average. The score=m^0.5*g^0.5 blend correctly keeps the min in control while letting the geometric mean break ties. This is the strongest 'is it compound' case in the batch.
C07.double_counting_risk: REAL and must be fixed before shipping. C11 (prospect-refuge, seat-level) ALREADY internalizes enclosure/refuge, yet enclosure index is fed again as a separate input; and the '(1-total_load)' multiplier is presumably built from glare risk + absorption, which are ALSO listed as standalone base measures. So refuge is counted via both C11 and enclosure, and glare/absorption are counted via both the load term and their own slots — the min-gate dampens but does not eliminate the double-weight. Fix: pick ONE representative per sensory channel (privacy=C7, distraction=C8, refuge=C11 OR enclosure not both, visual-comfort=glare-in-load not glare-standalone).
C07.citation_concern: None material. Hongisto 2005 genuinely shows acoustic cost is driven by worst-case intelligibility exposure (supports the min-gate on the acoustic leg); Banbury & Berry 2005 (irrelevant-speech effect), Appleton 1975 (prospect-refuge), Kim & de Dear 2013 (open-plan noise/privacy as top dissatisfier) are all real and on-point. Anchors support the structure, not just the topic.
C07.fixed_computation: Deduplicate inputs to one-per-channel (privacy, distraction, refuge, traffic, visual-comfort load), then m=min over the deduped gated qualities, score=sqrt(m)*sqrt(geomean). Keep ABSTAIN on privacy without plan/STI. Region-A-vs-B claim (perimeter protected-back desk vs central cross-path desk dragged to ~0.25 by the privacy min) follows correctly from the min-gate.
C07.reasons: Real non-additive weakest-link interaction, evidence supports the min structure not just the topic, computable on Tier A+B. Ceiling AMBER is correct (rides inferred plan/STI). Only defect is input double-counting between C11 and enclosure/glare/absorption, which is a fix, not a kill.

C08.is_it_really_compound: YES, mostly. The multiplicative gate curvature x (1-enclosure) x (1-clutter) is genuinely more than parts: a curved but fully enclosed volume, or a curved but visually chaotic one, both collapse — the openness and coherence factors are not cosmetic. The value is not a disguised average. Caveat: curvature is doing most of the lifting and the warmth term is a bounded modulator only.
C08.double_counting_risk: Two flags. (1) INTERNAL: 'curvature/rectilinearity index' and 'contour angularity' plus the 'sharp-angularity veto' are the same underlying contour signal read up to three times (once as the driving factor, once as a veto). Defensible ONLY if the mapping is non-monotone (moderate curve rewards, sharp angle vetoes) rather than linear-repeated — state that explicitly or the veto is redundant. (2) EXTERNAL: the Vartanian curvature->approach signal is essentially what the fluency->affect hedonics layer already encodes, so compounding curvature here re-weights a signal already scored downstream. Ensure this field is used as a spatial 'where do people advance' field, not summed back into the global hedonic score.
C08.citation_concern: Anchors are legitimate and, unusually, the social/behavioural claim is supported: Vartanian et al. 2013 (PNAS) did measure approach-avoidance ('enter' vs 'exit') decisions for curved vs rectilinear interiors, so 'drives enter decisions' is not overclaimed. Bar & Neta 2006 (sharp contour -> threat/amygdala) and Coburn et al. 2020 support the veto. Good.
C08.fixed_computation: Collapse curvature + contour-angularity into a single non-monotone contour term (reward mid-curvature, hard veto only past a sharpness threshold) so the signal is not triple-counted, then field=(contour*openness*coherence)*(1+0.5*warm). A-vs-B (curved open reception nucleates vs rectilinear low-soffit corridor mouth passes through) follows from the gate.
C08.reasons: Genuine conjunctive gate, Tier-A computable, and the approach evidence actually measured approach behaviour. AMBER correct (proxy-grade approach link + heuristic segmentation). Contour-signal redundancy and external double-count with the hedonics layer are fixable, not fatal.

C09.is_it_really_compound: YES. resource=(prospect x daylight)*(1-glare_gate) is a true sign-flipping antagonism: identical view depth yields opposite dwell because glare co-locates as a veto cost. An average would report 'moderate' for the exact case (high view + high glare) where the real outcome is near-zero occupancy. This is precisely the kind of interaction single primitives miss.
C09.double_counting_risk: Moderate. prospect (view depth), daylight/vertical-illuminance proxy, AND glass material coverage all rise monotonically with aperture size, so prospect x daylight partly multiplies aperture by itself. Use glass coverage only inside the glare_gate scaler (as specified) and not as a fourth positive term, and treat prospect and daylight as the two semi-independent legs, to avoid squaring window-size.
C09.citation_concern: Anchors real and correctly typed: Appleton 1975 (prospect), Jakubiec & Reinhart 2012 (Daylight Glare Probability / adaptive-zone DGP — a genuine glare metric, not a preference proxy), Osterhaus 2005 (discomfort-glare review), Boubekri et al. 1991 (window/sunlight penetration and well-being). No preference-sold-as-stress problem here.
C09.fixed_computation: Keep resource=sqrt(prospect*daylight); glare_gate=sigmoid past a discomfort threshold scaled by glass*(1-shade_cue); value=resource*(1-glare_gate). CRITICAL computability caveat to record: glare risk from a single photo is confounded by camera auto-exposure (a window is often blown out regardless of true UGR/DGP) and shade-cue detection is fragile — hence AMBER is mandatory and the field should lean toward ABSTAIN when exposure metadata is missing. A-vs-B (north-shaded perch dwelt-in vs west-glary identical-view seat empty at peak sun) follows from the veto.
C09.reasons: True antagonism with a documented sign flip, real glare metrics behind it, Tier-A. The single-photo glare confound is the one substantive risk and is correctly capped at AMBER; keep it as a compound but guard the glare leg.

C10.is_it_really_compound: YES structurally — a mid-fractal inverted-U CONDITIONED on a coherence gate with a discomfort veto is non-monotone and sign-flipping (the same raw richness helps when organized, repels when not). That conditioning is a real interaction, not a mean.
C10.double_counting_risk: THE dominant concern in this candidate, and it is serious.
