Fractal dimension, colour-palette entropy, and Rosenholtz clutter are three CORRELATED complexity measures; the formula arbitrarily assigns fractal='complexity', entropy+symmetry='coherence', clutter=separate — but entropy is itself a complexity/disorder measure being repurposed as coherence, which is dubious. Worse: the 'spectral discomfort deviation' (deviation from a 1/f amplitude spectrum, Fernandez & Wilkins) is mathematically near-tautological with fractal dimension, since the fractal dimension of an image is derived from the SAME amplitude-spectrum slope. So the 'complexity band' term and the 'discomfort veto' term risk reading one physical property twice, and fractal already feeds the hedonics inverted-U layer downstream — triple exposure of the spectral signal.
C10.citation_concern: Anchors themselves are real (Kaplan & Kaplan 1989 complexity-needs-coherence; Taylor 2011 / Hagerhall 2004 mid-fractal ~1.3-1.5 preference; Reber et al. 2004 fluency; Fernandez & Wilkins 2008 discomfort from non-1/f images). Note most are PREFERENCE/comfort findings, so the 'restoration/dwell' outcome sign is proxy-grade, not a physiological result — keep AMBER and do not sell as a stress/biosignal claim without the survey->biosignal->physical triplet.
C10.fixed_computation: Disentangle the spectral overlap: if fractal D and the 1/f-deviation are both computed from the amplitude spectrum, use ONE spectral term (either the band OR the deviation-veto, not both as independent factors) and source the coherence gate from a genuinely orthogonal cue (horizontal symmetry / edge-alignment), not from entropy. Then value=band(D) * symmetry_coherence * (1-clutter), emit overload_flag. A-vs-B (rhythmic symmetric gallery wall slows people vs equal-richness mismatched ductwork speeds them up) still follows.
C10.reasons: Coherence-gated non-monotone fascination is a legitimate compound and Tier-A computable, but it is one algebraic step from double-counting the amplitude-spectrum slope (fractal vs 1/f-deviation) and from re-weighting fractal already in the hedonics layer. Survives as VIABLE only conditional on the spectral-term disentangling above; AMBER is correct.

C11.is_it_really_compound: YES on paper — a threshold-gated PRODUCT (sittability x prospect x back-refuge) correctly zeroes a wall-facing bench (high seat provision, zero prospect) where a mean would report 'moderate'. The compound logic is sound and it exposes exactly the code-masking (seat COUNT met by seats facing a wall) the brief cares about.
C11.double_counting_risk: Minor: enclosure index supplies 'back-refuge' and also bounds prospect; keep enclosure on the refuge leg only. The larger problem is not double-counting but a base-measure/computation MISMATCH — the interaction form is written over {sittability, prospect, back-refuge} yet the declared base_measures are {prospect, enclosure, acoustic absorption proxy}. 'Sittability' is never defined as an input and 'acoustic absorption proxy' never appears in the computation. The spec is internally inconsistent.
C11.citation_concern: None — anchors are top-tier and correctly social: Whyte 1980 (sittable space dominates plaza use), Gehl 1987/2011 (edge effect), Appleton 1975 (prospect). This is the best-evidenced candidate.
C11.fixed_computation: Two required inputs before it can produce a non-abstaining number: (1) a seatable-plane / seat-height detector — classical CV cannot reliably identify a ~40-50cm sittable ledge from a monocular photo (needs metric scale, which the photo lacks -> monocular scale ambiguity), so this must come from Tier-B declared seat geometry or a 3D model, and on pure Tier-A it will ABSTAIN nearly always; (2) reconcile the dangling acoustic-absorption input (either drop it or make it a fourth gated leg for 'found-not-overheard'). With a declared seat map it becomes a clean product-gate. A-vs-B (planter ledge back-to-wall facing entry stream occupied vs backless open-middle stools empty) is correct.
C11.reasons: Excellent Whyte/Gehl grounding and a correct product-gate, but it is not viable as an autonomous Tier-A photo attribute: it fundamentally needs a seat-plane / seatable-surface input the photo cannot supply, and its base-measure list does not match its own computation. Resolve the seat-geometry input and the absorption mismatch and it upgrades to VIABLE (AMBER). Concept is good — hence not a KILL.

C12.is_it_really_compound: YES — a three-way spatial co-occurrence (sun-patch ∩ sittable ∩ within r of amenity) with a THERMAL sign-gate flipping sun from resource to cost is a genuine non-additive, context-dependent interaction, exactly the timing/sign-flip pattern the brief wants. Two-of-three low collapses it; an average would not.
C12.double_counting_risk: Not classic double-counting, but a VALIDITY substitution: 'landmark salience' is used to stand in for 'food/amenity anchor'. Whyte's engine is specifically FOOD + seating + sun; a generic saliency proxy fires on a sculpture or a bright sign as readily as a coffee cart, so the amenity leg is measuring the wrong construct. And, as in C11, 'sittability' appears in the computation but is not in the base-measure list.
C12.citation_concern: Anchors are real (Whyte 1980 sun+food+seating as strongest use correlates; Nikolopoulou & Lykoudis 2006 outdoor thermal adaptation/comfort; Gehl 1987/2011). But the computation under-delivers on Whyte because landmark salience does not identify FOOD — so the literature is invoked more tightly than the computable inputs actually support. Flag as a construct-mapping stretch.
C12.fixed_computation: Requires multiple declared inputs the photo cannot give: (1) sun-patch geometry — a single photo is a snapshot and cannot yield the daily/seasonal sun path, so this needs a declared 3D + solar-position model (correctly self-flagged, tier=3D_model, ABSTAIN on photo); (2) a real thermal-comfort input (weather/declared, not a photo proxy); (3) an amenity/POI map that actually tags food/service, replacing landmark salience; (4) a seatable-plane detector as in C11. With those, value = overlap-area product x sign(thermal_comfort) in [-1,+1] is clean. A-vs-B (cool-day sunlit ledge 2m from a coffee cart dense vs shaded seats 40m from amenity empty, flipping in a July heatwave) is a correct consequence of the sign-gate.
C12.reasons: The interaction and thermal sign-flip are genuinely valuable and real, but the attribute stands on four inputs the photo cannot supply (solar/3D sun-patch, thermal, amenity-semantics, seat detection) and substitutes landmark salience for the food anchor Whyte actually requires. Low feasibility (rank 5.5) is honest. Not a KILL — with a declared 3D/solar model + thermal input + a true amenity map it is a legitimate AMBER compound — but as specified it needs those extra inputs to compute what it claims.

C01.is_it_really_compound: Yes — genuinely compound. The value comes strictly from CO-LOCATION: a threshold gate on the anchor's normal distance to the max-integration desire-line ridge. Salience alone or integration alone cannot fire it, and a plain average of salience and integration would wrongly score the dead-alcove fountain highly; the gate correctly zeros it. The interaction is real, not a disguised sum.
C01.double_counting_risk: landmark salience and C1 integration also feed the hedonics/plan-score layer; if this field is ALSO summed into the aggregate score, both signals get re-weighted. Keep it as its own social field, not added back into the general valence score.
C01.citation_concern: Whyte 1980 (triangulation prop must lie where people cross) and Hillier et al. 1993 (integration predicts natural movement) are both real and accurately characterized. No overclaim; neither is a social study sold as physiological.
C01.fixed_computation: Require the anchor to be detected in photo-salience AND registered to a plan cell above a confidence threshold; abstain (UNKNOWN) when cross-tier registration confidence is low rather than guessing the centroid.
C01.reasons: Flagship of David's core social lever and the cleanest compound in the set. Computable NOW at Tier B. Chief residual risk is cross-tier registration: you must place a photo-detected salient anchor onto a single-photo-INFERRED plan grid and overlay it on a VGA ridge derived from that same inferred plan — a stack of heuristics, correctly capped at AMBER. Region-A-vs-B (cross-path fountain ignites vs dead-alcove fountain admired) follows directly from the gate. I would defend this to Kirsh.

C02.is_it_really_compound: Yes — triangulation pull (salience x integration/path-overlap) MULTIPLIED by an acoustic-hearability gate (STI_local>0.5). Pull without hearability = 0 and hearability without pull = 0; the acoustic term is a distinct signal from the pull, so it is a real compound extension of C01, not a rename.
C02.double_counting_risk: Reuses the same salience x integration pull as C01 and the same absorption proxy as C04/C05. If both C01 and C02 feed a downstream score, triangulation pull is counted twice. The acoustic gate is the only new information here.
C02.citation_concern: Hongisto 2005 is primarily a distraction/performance-vs-STI model (rising speech intelligibility raises distraction), NOT a study of the STI floor needed to SUSTAIN a two-talker conversation. The physical requirement is uncontroversial, but crediting it to Hongisto 2005 is a mild mischaracterization — cite a direct two-talker communication/STI source.
C02.reasons: Genuine compound, but STI_local is estimated from absorption + enclosure proxies that are themselves inferred from a photo — a proxy of a proxy. Its ceiling is therefore permanently AMBER; it can never validate without a real ISO 3382-3 measurement. Viable and useful for the mature-vs-abort distinction, but the amber cap is structural, not incidental.

C03.is_it_really_compound: Yes, and the strongest interaction design in the set: the sociofugal veto multiplicatively zeros the whole product regardless of Hall-distance, barrier, or intelligibility. Combined with the Hall-band bump and an inverted-U on C3, it is emphatically non-additive — exactly the kind of interaction the brief wants.
C03.double_counting_risk: C1 integration and C3 intelligibility are reused from the plan-metric layer; low concern relative to the input gap.
C03.citation_concern: Anchors are real and on-point: Osmond 1957 (sociopetal/sociofugal), Sommer & Ross 1958 (facing-in roughly doubled conversation — a genuine SOCIAL-interaction study, correctly used), Hall 1966, Mehrabian & Diamond 1971. Evidence layer is strong and honestly social.
C03.reasons: The blocker is input, not logic. It requires recoverable seat FACING vectors; social.sociopetal sits in the cataloged-but-uncomputed registry, and chair orientation/pose from a single photo needs semantics classical CV does not reliably provide. The candidate honestly abstains without seat orientation, which means in current practice it abstains most of the time. Genuinely valuable once a seat/orientation map exists — hence NEEDS_EXTRA_INPUT rather than KILL.

C04.is_it_really_compound: Yes — product of complements: refuge x (1-STI_to_others) x (1-integration/exposure) x absorption. Conjunctive: any single high-exposure term drives it to zero, so it is a true multiplicative interaction (all seclusion conditions must co-hold), not an average of privacy-ish scores.
C04.double_counting_risk: refuge/enclosure, integration, and absorption are shared with C05/C06 and it is the geometric mirror of C01/C06, so the plan-integration signal recurs across the family. Manage shared-input weighting at the score layer.
C04.citation_concern: Sailer & McCulloh 2012 (segregated locations host longer uninterrupted interaction — real, social), Osmond/Sommer, Appleton 1975 (prospect-refuge), ISO 3382-3, Alexander et al. 1977 (alcoves/intimacy gradient) all real and correctly applied. No overclaim.
C04.reasons: The candidate itself declares tier=extra_input and is right to: the STI_to_others privacy term needs a material/absorption table or a declared 3D model and abstains from a bare photo. The geometry sub-part (refuge x segregation) is computable as SCORED-partial, but full value — the whole point, acoustic seclusion — requires the extra acoustic input. Honest design (partial-geometry SCORED vs privacy UNKNOWN). NEEDS_EXTRA_INPUT.

C05.is_it_really_compound: Yes — conjunctive masking detector: affect_good (warm/wood/fluency) x reverb_bad (hard+specular, low-absorption), firing only when BOTH exceed threshold. The product of a POSITIVE perceptual signal with a NEGATIVE physical one is precisely the masking signature and cannot be recovered from either primitive alone. (Minor: reverb_bad internally sums two hardness proxies — an acceptable within-term aggregation; the headline interaction remains a product.)
C05.double_counting_risk: IMPORTANT: the affect_good term literally reuses the fluency->affect signal that already feeds the hedonics layer, and absorption/warm-cool/wood feed it too. This is fine ONLY because the output is a divergence FLAG, not a score contributor. It must never be added into the positive valence score, or it double-weights affect. Keep strictly diagnostic.
C05.citation_concern: Peelle 2018 (listening effort is a measurable physiological load even at maintained comprehension — a genuine biosignal-level finding, not a preference study), Pichora-Fuller et al. 2016 (FUEL), Reber/Winkielman/Schwarz 1998/2004 (fluency->affect) are all real and correctly characterized. No preference-sold-as-stress error.
C05.reasons: The exemplary masking compound and a textbook subjective-vs-physical divergence, computable at Tier A from the photo material palette. Directly implements the doc's cardinal masking trap. Ceiling AMBER is honest because RT60/absorption is a photo proxy and true validity needs the subjective->biosignal->physical triplet. Strong; I would defend it.

C06.is_it_really_compound: Yes — a ratio, and the division is what makes the meaning RELATIONAL: the same integration reads as 'spine' when refuge/enclosure are low and 'dwell node' when high. An average of movement and stay potentials would collapse that distinction; the ratio preserves it. Genuine compound, correctly a discriminator rather than a valence.
C06.double_counting_risk: Heavy, and internal to the compound: the numerator multiplies THREE correlated through-movement proxies (I integration, K connectivity, p path-overlap all covary), inflating the movement side; the denominator multiplies two correlated enclosure proxies (refuge x enclosure). The direction is sound but the magnitude is over-driven by redundant inputs. Also I/K are reused across C01-C04.
C06.citation_concern: Hillier 1996 (through-movement/choice vs to-movement/integration), Gehl 2010 (movement vs staying), Turner 2001 (VGA), Whyte 1980 all real and correctly used.
C06.fixed_computation: Replace I*K*p with a single decorrelated movement score (e.g., betweenness/choice) and refuge*enclosure with one dwell/enclosure score, then keep the ratio. This removes the intra-term double-counting while preserving the relational discriminator.
C06.reasons: Genuinely useful ratio compound for optimiser furniture placement (spine vs one-step-off dwell node), computable at Tier B. Kept off VIABLE only by the correlated-input inflation on both sides of the ratio, which should be orthogonalized before the number is trusted quantitatively. Region-A-vs-B follows from the ratio. AMBER cap appropriate (rides inferred plan).

C19.is_it_really_compound: Yes — genuine super-additive product O*Rng*Pull. Overlap without range = anonymous passing; proximity without shared paths = silent neighbours; only the conjunction yields repeated range-close crossings. The value lives in the interaction, not an average.
C19.double_counting_risk: Pull term is C23 commons-gravity used as one input; not re-weighted elsewhere in this attribute. Low risk. Watch that C6 path-overlap and C5 proximity are computed on the SAME inferred plan so the product isn't two views of one geometry.
C19.citation_concern: Clean — Kabo et al. 2014 genuinely shows bump-space overlap predicts new collaboration BEYOND raw proximity (the exact super-additive claim); Allen 1977 curve real. Social claim rests on a social study. Good.
C19.reasons: Strong, well-evidenced compound. The Allen-curve range gate on path overlap is exactly the interaction Kabo isolates. Region A-vs-B (overlapping ~10 m corridor to shared coffee vs non-overlapping single-team approaches) follows directly from the computation. Correctly ABSTAINS on C5 without a seat/team map, which is what forces the AMBER ceiling. Defensible to Kirsh.

C20.is_it_really_compound: Yes — inverted-U RT60 band × enclosure × 1/spacing is genuinely non-additive; a group both under- (dead) and over-reverberant fails, and the peak only helps when compact and shielded. Not an average.
C20.double_counting_risk: Real but non-fatal: 'acoustic absorption proxy' and 'material coverage (hard surfaces)' are two names for the same plane-material segmentation, so RT60_proxy is derived from ONE acoustic signal, not two. The cross-family compound survives because the interaction is RT60-band × enclosure × spacing, three independent axes.
C20.citation_concern: Mild stretch: Bradley/ANSI S12.60 evidence is single-talker classroom intelligibility; a 3-6 person multi-talker huddle is an extrapolation. Bronkhorst 2000 supports the multi-talker direction. Proxy-grade link, honestly AMBER.
C20.reasons: Distinct from the privacy fields (it wants reverberation IN-band and inward reach, the opposite optimum to confidentiality). Genuine interaction, but absorption/hard-coverage redundancy should be stated so the RT60 proxy isn't sold as two evidence sources, and the huddle application of classroom data is proxy-grade. ABSTAINS without Tier-B cluster. AMBER correct.

C21.is_it_really_compound: As written, NO — this is the disguised-single-measure trap. C8 distraction distance rD is DEFINED (ISO 3382-3) as the radius where STI reaches a fixed value (~0.5). Sampling STI_out AT rD pins the denominator to that constant, so STI_in/STI_out reduces to STI_in rescaled — a single measure wearing two names — and/or a restatement of C8 itself. The gradient is real only if the two radii are chosen independently of the STI value.
C21.double_counting_risk: Severe overlap with C8: rD IS the inverse gradient, so a gradient built on rD re-derives C8. The prior committee's parenthetical 'borderline overlap with C8 (noted)' understates this — it is potentially the whole attribute.
C21.fixed_computation: Sample STI at two FIXED geometric radii independent of rD (e.g. STI_in at 1 m, STI_out at a fixed 4-5 m), gradient = STI_in/(STI_out+eps). Then the denominator varies with the actual acoustic field and the ratio genuinely captures decay steepness. With that respec it becomes a real compound and a valuable low-level/shallow-gradient mask detector.
C21.citation_concern: ISO 3382-3:2012, Haapakangas/Hongisto, Sörqvist 2010 (irrelevant-speech effect) all real and on-point. Evidence is fine; the defect is computational, not citational.
C21.reasons: The IDEA (gradient steepness makes a space simultaneously private and focus-friendly, and exposes the quiet-level/carrying-speech mask) is genuinely valuable. But the specified computation, tying the outer radius to C8's definitional rD, risks collapsing to a rescaled single STI or to C8. Requires the two-fixed-radii respec above before it can ship; not a KILL, but not viable as written.

C22.is_it_really_compound: Yes — occ^2 × (1-absorption) × (1-enclosure) is genuinely super-additive: competing-talker babble accumulates far faster when the envelope neither absorbs nor shields. The multiplication of occupancy-nonlinearity with acoustic leakage is the signal.
C22.double_counting_risk: Distinct from C20/C21 (babble build-up vs group intelligibility vs decay steepness). Low risk. Ensure absorption and enclosure are drawn once and not re-weighted.
C22.citation_concern: Cherry 1953, Bronkhorst 2000, Hongisto 2005 all real and on-topic. One honesty note: the SQUARE exponent on occupancy is a modelling choice, not a literature-fixed value — SNR degradation with talker count is documented as nonlinear/monotone but the specific power of 2 is heuristic. State it as 'nonlinear (illustratively squared)' rather than an empirical exponent.
C22.reasons: Genuine multiplicative interaction with correct sign (raising it worsens conversation initiation, pushing toward fleeting/retreat). Region A-vs-B at the SAME occupancy (absorptive nook: normal-voice conversation starts; hard hall: only shouted exchanges) follows cleanly and controls for density, which is the sharp part. ABSTAINS without occupancy grid. AMBER right; flag the exponent as heuristic.

C23.is_it_really_compound: Yes, and the form is correct: a threshold-gated MINIMUM (Liebig weakest-link) is exactly right for fear cues, which combine as a binding constraint, not a sum. One unlit corner or blocked exit collapses the field regardless of the other factors — an average would wrongly let a bright open plan compensate for a blocked exit.
C23.double_counting_risk: The min form structurally resists double-weighting (it returns the binding factor, not a sum), so even though enclosure/prospect also feed other layers, no signal is silently doubled here. Low risk.
C23.citation_concern: Strongest evidence set of the batch: Fisher & Nasar 1992 is an actual fear-of-crime study showing concealment+prospect+escape COMBINE (not a preference study sold as a stress result); Appleton 1975, Blöbaum & Hunecke 2005 on-point. Safety claim rests on safety studies. Clean.
C23.reasons: Correct non-additive form, correct sign (a low value suppresses dwell/approach for any occupant), and it is honestly framed as the enabling FLOOR beneath every social form. Minor spec nit: the named base measure 'brightness variance' is global while the computation uses min local luminance in the lower periphery — reconcile the named input with the lighting-floor term. ABSTAINS on escape without inferred plan → AMBER. rank 8.5 justified; best of the batch.

C24.is_it_really_compound: The NEW interaction — a crowding-KNEE gate on a safety-conditioned dwell term — is genuinely compound and threshold-conditional. But as written the attribute also multiplies redundant constituents, so most of its factors are not adding independent information.
C24.double_counting_risk: SEVERE and undersold by the prior committee: felt_safety_field (C23) ALREADY contains prospect and enclosure/refuge, yet C24 re-multiplies by C11 (prospect-refuge) AND by standalone prospect (view depth). That triple-counts prospect and double-counts refuge, silently over-weighting the prospect/refuge signal by 2-3x. A naive implementation returns a wrong (over-weighted) number.
C24.fixed_computation: Strip the redundant terms: LA = felt_safety_field × (C12<knee ? 1 : 1-(C12-knee)/(1-knee)). Optionally retain a SEAT-level refuge term ONLY if it carries information not already in C23's refuge (e.g. chair-back presence from the photo, distinct from architectural enclosure). Drop the duplicate prospect and C11 factors. The genuinely novel content is the crowding-knee gate on safety — keep exactly that.
C24.citation_concern: Whyte 1980, Gehl 2011 (edge effect), Marcus & Francis 1997 all real and support dwell-drives-social-life. Evidence fine; the defect is the double-count, not the citations.
C24.reasons: Conceptually sound and distinct from C23 (adds a real crowding threshold interaction), but ships a formula bug: it re-multiplies prospect and refuge that C23 already encodes, over-weighting them. Viable only after de-duplication per fixed_computation; with that, the safety×crowding-knee gate is a legitimate AMBER compound. Region A-vs-B (backed perimeter bench vs exposed island seat) follows. AMBER, conditional on the fix.

C13.is_it_really_compound: Yes, genuinely compound and non-additive. The value is base(F*P*V) multiplied by an inverted-U crowding term m=crowd*(1-crowd)/0.25 that peaks at moderate density and collapses at both extremes. Neither raw flow nor raw pinch nor raw crowding predicts the peak alone; the signal lives in the product-times-nonmonotone-gate. Strongest interaction form in the set.
C13.double_counting_risk: C12 crowding enters twice conceptually (it is density-derived, and threshold density is also a base measure), but it is re-expressed through a non-monotone transform rather than re-summed, so it is not silent double-weighting. Mild flag only.
C13.citation_concern: Whyte 1980 self-congestion, Gehl and Hillier are real. But the SPECIFIC inverted-U crowding penalty (comfort knee) is a modeling assumption, not a Whyte measurement; Whyte documented conversation clustering mid-stream, not the descending limb. Label the knee as a proxy calibration, not an observed curve.
C13.fixed_computation: Name the two unnamed inputs: mutual visibility V and pinch-narrowing P are used in the computation but are NOT in the declared base-measure list (C6, threshold density, C12). V should be sourced explicitly from C1/isovist and P from plan geometry; per honesty constraint 1 every input must be named or the attribute abstains.
C13.reasons: Best-evidenced, most genuinely non-monotone interaction here; Tier-B computable on the inferred plan; high A-vs-B value (pinch vs even corridor follows from the P term). AMBER is correct because it rides inferred plan and a calibrated (not observed) crowding knee. Fix: declare V and P as named inputs.

C14.is_it_really_compound: Compound via spatial co-occurrence: the per-anchor product s_i*I_i rewards only anchors that are salient AND on the integrated network, and the ratio measures what fraction of attractor mass lands on paths. An off-path sculpture inflates the denominator but not the numerator. Caveat: with a single detected anchor R degenerates to that anchor's integration (a single measure); the compound character only emerges with multiple anchors, so it must abstain or flag when only one anchor is found.
C14.double_counting_risk: C24 awe is used only as the external comparison term for masking detection, not inside R, so no double-weighting in the ratio itself. Clean.
C14.citation_concern: Whyte 1980 triangulation and Hillier & Hanson 1984 configurational social yield are real and on-point. No overclaim.
C14.reasons: Standout masking-diagnostic: it catches decoration-as-social-generosity that inflates awe/richness while producing zero contact, which no single primitive sees, and it directly guards the score layer's honesty. Computability is AMBER because it needs discrete anchor localization onto the plan grid (saliency maps to plan cells), which is heuristic and needs semantics classical CV only approximates. Add the single-anchor degeneracy guard.

C15.is_it_really_compound: Yes: a conjunctive min-gate times a product, min(sociofugal, refuge)*(1-path_overlap)*(1-STI). A plush high-refuge seat on a busy desire line fails the path-overlap term and collapses; a quiet seat facing inward fails the sociofugal term. Failing any single component drags the whole product down, a real Liebig-style interaction, not an average.
C15.double_counting_risk: Its four inputs (sociopetal, C11, C6, C7) each also feed other scores, but they are combined conjunctively into a distinct construct (retreat) rather than re-summed into an existing one; acceptable.
C15.citation_concern: Sommer 1966 (solo readers chose back-to-wall/corner), Appleton 1975, Hall 1966 all real and correctly applied.
C15.reasons: Distinct human effect (see-without-being-engaged) and distinct interaction form (min-gate) from the huddle/co-awareness fields; scores oppositely to sociopetal clusters by construction, giving genuine A-vs-B value. Honestly abstains without seat orientation, an acceptable design under constraint 1. AMBER because it needs inferred seat facing.

C16.is_it_really_compound: The interaction is genuine and is the best-motivated agency-buffer in the set: the same intimate-distance stimulus scores very differently depending on the multiplicative (1-barrier)*(1-control) attenuation. Not an average.
C16.double_counting_risk: Low; the components are combined into a new construct.
C16.citation_concern: Evans & Wener 2007 (commuter proximity raised crowding and cortisol) and Evans & Stecker 2004 (agency buffer) are real and correctly cited as STRESS/physiology findings, not preference. Good.
C16.fixed_computation: The step function fires only below ~0.45 m, a HARD METRIC threshold, but monocular photos and heuristic vanishing-point plans carry severe scale ambiguity at that resolution; a 0.45 m step is fragile against scale error. More decisively, firing requires detecting occupant PAIRS and their pairwise metric distances and facing directions - a real seat/occupancy map that Tier A/B do not provide and that is not in the declared base palette. Control also abstains unless declared.
C16.reasons: Interaction and evidence are solid, but this cannot compute on the declared palette: it needs (a) a person/occupancy-pair map, (b) reliable metric scale for the intimate step, (c) seat orientation, and (d) declared control. Three of these are inputs the photo/inferred plan do not carry, so it would abstain in nearly all real cases. Not a KILL (the construct is valid) but gated behind occupancy + metric-scale inputs it does not yet have. Lowest rank in the set is consistent with this.

C17.is_it_really_compound: Marginally, and only under one reading. The claimed mask (high centrality, low realized visibility) requires C1 and prospect/isovist to be SEPARABLE. But VGA integration (C1) is itself computed from the visibility graph, so C1 and isovist prospect are largely the same underlying signal; multiplying them risks squaring visibility rather than capturing an interaction. It survives only because global configurational centrality (graph position across the whole plan) genuinely differs from the LOCAL directional isovist toward occupiable seats - a desk connected around a corner can be graph-central yet have a low forward isovist to occupied planes.
C17.double_counting_risk: REAL and must be flagged: C1 (VGA) and prospect are both visibility-graph-derived and both feed the score/hedonics layer; compounding them re-weights visibility twice. Mitigate by restricting the V term strictly to the DIRECTIONAL isovist toward occupiable planes (not omnidirectional openness), so it is orthogonal to global integration.
C17.citation_concern: Backhouse & Drew 1992 (interaction tracks mutual visibility, not proximity), Rashid et al. 2006, Benedikt 1979 all real and apt.
C17.fixed_computation: value = C1_global * V_directional_to_occupiable, where V is explicitly the forward isovist restricted to occupiable planes, NOT total openness, to avoid squaring the visibility signal.
C17.reasons: Clean central-but-blind mask with strong evidence and clear A-vs-B (integrated open desk vs integrated desk behind a partition). Downgraded from its self-assigned top rank because of the C1/isovist collinearity: the interaction is real only for the global-centrality-vs-local-directional-visibility distinction, which must be enforced in code or it degenerates into visibility-squared. AMBER.

C18.is_it_really_compound: The distinctive contribution - the Gaussian inverted-U on integration (moderate integration preferred: findable but not over-exposed) - IS a genuine, novel interaction not present in the other seat fields. That part is defensible and compound.
C18.double_counting_risk: SERIOUS as written: prospect enters TWICE (raw prospect term, and again inside C11 which is prospect-refuge), and refuge enters TWICE (inside C11, and again as the standalone back-refuge Bk term). So prospect^~2 * refuge^~2 * gauss(C1) silently double-weights prospect and refuge. This is the exact averaging/double-counting trap the constraints forbid.
C18.citation_concern: Appleton 1975 and Gehl 1987 are apt. But attributing the inverted-U 'being-found needs MODERATE integration' to Hillier & Hanson 1984 is an extrapolation - H&H link integration to movement/co-presence, not a documented over-exposure penalty; label the descending limb a modeling inference, not an H&H finding.
C18.fixed_computation: Collapse the redundant terms: value = C11(prospect-refuge) * gauss(C1), dropping the standalone raw prospect and standalone Bk terms since C11 already carries both. This preserves the distinct inverted-U signal while eliminating the prospect/refuge double-count. After the fix it is cleanly distinct from C17 (inverted-U vs monotone).
C18.reasons: Not a KILL because the inverted-U on integration is a real, distinct interaction that directly models David's lone-waiter form and yields a clean A-vs-B (wall bench facing entrance vs exposed open-middle stool). But it must ship with the de-duplication fix, or it double-weights prospect and refuge. AMBER, conditional on the fixed computation.

C25.is_it_really_compound: YES. Polarity = WaiterScore - HuddleScore where each pole is a TRIPLE PRODUCT (prospect*findability*back) and (refuge*(1-exposure)*(1-STI)). A difference of two genuine multiplicative terms is not linear in the primitives, so the value survives as an interaction, not an average. The antagonism (one region can serve waiter XOR huddle, not both) is exactly David's stated waiter-vs-huddle contrast, made into one signed field.
C25.double_counting_risk: Mild. prospect+enclosure already combine in C11 prospect-refuge and the hedonic layer; C25 recombines the same primitives. Safe ONLY if C25 is consumed as a standalone zone-assignment diagnostic (as positioned) and NOT summed back into the hedonic score alongside C11. Also the two poles are not fully orthogonal: enclosure enters positively in BOTH the waiter's 'protected back' and the huddle's 'refuge', so it is prospect/integration/STI (not enclosure) that carries the antagonism.
C25.citation_concern: Appleton 1975 is cited for 'prospect and refuge sometimes opposed', but Appleton's thesis is their COMPLEMENTARITY (see without being seen), not opposition. The opposition/antagonism finding is really Gatersleben & Andrews 2013. Lean on Gatersleben & Andrews for the sign, treat Appleton as background. Osmond/Sommer for seating orientation is correct.
C25.reasons: Genuine difference-of-products compound directly operationalising the brief's core waiter-vs-huddle antagonism; STI/orientation require inferred plan so it honestly abstains at Tier B → AMBER. The A-vs-B claim (entrance high-integration positive pole vs recessed low-STI alcove negative pole) follows directly from the signed field. Defensible to Kirsh at AMBER; fix the Appleton attribution and keep it out of the additive hedonic aggregate.

C26.is_it_really_compound: NO — this is the decisive failure. Expand it: GAP = mean(illum, prospect, 1-clutter) - [1 - mean(occ, entrap, 1-corner)] = mean(illum,prospect,1-clutter) + mean(occ,entrap,1-corner) - 1. That is a plain WEIGHTED SUM of six base measures. There is no product, ratio, threshold, conditional, or co-occurrence anywhere — both composites are arithmetic means and their difference stays linear. A masking gap must DETECT the conjunction 'looks-safe HIGH while physical-safe LOW'; a linear difference cannot — a bland medium-everything cell scores identically to the genuinely dangerous high-look/low-physical cell whenever the sums match. It is a disguised average wearing a two-register costume.
C26.double_counting_risk: Prospect appears on BOTH sides (directly in looks_safe, inversely via occlusivity/entrapment in physical_safe), so the 'gap' is partly a repackaged single openness axis rather than a true cross-register divergence.
C26.citation_concern: Fisher & Nasar 1992 and Nasar, Fisher & Grannis 1993 are perceived-fear/perception studies; Boomsma & Steg 2014 is perceived social safety. NONE measured physiology, yet the human_effect claims the mask 'still loads threat physiology'. That is a perception result sold as a biosignal result — an overclaim the brief's evidence rule forbids. A physiological-masking claim needs an actual SUBJECTIVE→BIOSIGNAL→PHYSICAL triplet, not three perception surveys.
C26.fixed_computation: Reformulate as an explicit conjunction so the interaction is real: MASK = relu(looks_safe - t) * relu((1-physical_safe) - t), i.e. fires only when a bright/open surface read CO-OCCURS with a concealed/entrapping physical reality; then re-cite for the physiology claim or downgrade the claim to perceived-safety only. As a product-of-thresholded-terms it would become a genuine compound and could be reconsidered.
C26.reasons: Killed as specified: it is a linear sum of six primitives, not a compound, and the physiology effect is unsupported by the perception-only citations. Respectable KILL — the underlying masking intent is legitimate and endorsed by the brief, but the author must make the interaction real (conjunction, above) before resubmission.

C27.is_it_really_compound: YES. A conditional sign-flip: above the prospect knee refuge ADDS restorative value (prospect*refuge*(1+0.5*C19)); below it refuge is PENALIZED as entrapment (-(refuge-prospect)*occlusivity). Non-monotone in refuge, gated on prospect — a textbook conditional interaction, not an average. The same 'refuge' input yields opposite signs depending on prospect, which is the whole point.
C27.double_counting_risk: Low. C19 and prospect/enclosure feed other layers, but the conditional recombination is new structure and the output is a standalone restorative-suitability field, not an additive re-weighting.
C27.citation_concern: Clean. Gatersleben & Andrews 2013 genuinely found high-prospect/low-refuge most restorative and refuge-without-prospect (entrapment) reducing it — the computation encodes exactly that finding. Herzog & Kropscott 2004 (prospect/refuge/mystery, preference and perceived danger) is real and on-point.
C27.reasons: The candidate literally implements the Gatersleben-Andrews conditional; genuine sign-flip compound at Tier A, riding heuristic monocular depth/occlusivity → AMBER. A-vs-B (window-backed nook positive vs windowless enclosed recess negative/entrapment) follows directly from the branch logic. Defensible.

C28.is_it_really_compound: YES in structure — surveillance_by_others x own_refuge as a thresholded co-occurrence product (both-or-neither AND-gate): high surveillance x low refuge = exposure stress, high refuge x zero surveillance = isolation, only the joint cell reassures. That is a true multiplicative interaction that flags the 'refuge-alone-mistaken-for-safety' failure.
C28.citation_concern: Solid: Jacobs 1961 (eyes on the street), Newman 1972 (defensible space / natural surveillance), Loewen, Steel & Suedfeld 1993 (prospect/refuge and fear). The felt-safety-in-populated-setting construct is well anchored.
C28.reasons: Not computable on the declared Tier-A photo / Tier-B inferred-plan palette: 'surveillance by others' needs a real occupancy / people-in-view / gaze-orientation map that the single photo generally cannot supply (C1 gives only latent overseeing CAPACITY, not who is actually watching). The candidate honestly marks tier=extra_input and abstains without occupancy — so the correct verdict is NEEDS_EXTRA_INPUT: viable and well-grounded, but gated on an occupancy input beyond the base measures. The A-vs-B claim follows only once that input is present.

C29.is_it_really_compound: YES. index = (salience x fluency) / (eps + integration x connectivity) — a ratio of an appeal product to a movement product. Ratio and products are genuine interactions; the diagnostic value is the DIVERGENCE between 'looks inviting' and 'is on the flow', which no arithmetic average expresses. A pretty-but-off-the-lattice amenity is precisely a high numerator / low denominator cell.
C29.double_counting_risk: Present but tolerable. perceptual-fluency-affect feeds the hedonics layer and landmark salience feeds the landmark layer; C29 reuses both. This is safe ONLY because C29 is a standalone warning diagnostic (high = predicted under-used) rather than an additive contribution to the appeal score — if it were summed into the hedonic score it would double-weight fluency/salience. Flag to keep it segregated.
C29.citation_concern: Strong and directly on-point: Whyte 1980 documented attractive off-desire-line plazas sitting empty and triangulation/desire lines; Reber, Schwarz & Winkielman 2004 shows fluency inflates appeal (justifying why the numerator over-reads use). Gehl 2010 supports movement-lattice dependence. No overclaim.
C29.reasons: Genuine appeal/movement ratio catching the render-vs-use mask designers fall for; a high value correctly WORSENS the realised-encounter prediction. Rides Tier-B inferred integration/connectivity → AMBER. A-vs-B (furnished lounge at dead-corridor end high/empty vs plain bench on the spine low/used) follows directly from the ratio. Defensible at AMBER.

C30.is_it_really_compound: YES. Threshold-gated geometric mean (veg'*soft'*absorb'*day')^(1/4) with relu floors is a spatial AND-gate: any single missing ingredient (no greenery, hard/glary surfaces, overhead-only light) drives the term to ~0 in a way an arithmetic mean would not (a maxed-daylight bare grid collapses). That non-substitutability is a real multiplicative interaction, not an average.
C30.double_counting_risk: REAL — flag prominently. The 'soft-material composite' (specularity-inverse, texture homogeneity, wood/upholstery coverage) and the 'acoustic absorption proxy' are BOTH derived from the same material coverage table over planes; they are strongly correlated by construction. Multiplying them in the geometric mean over-weights material softness relative to the veg and daylight terms — a hidden double-count of one signal. Consider collapsing soft-material and absorption into one material-softness term, or down-weighting, before use.
C30.citation_concern: Acceptable but note grade: Coley, Kuo & Sullivan 1997 and Kuo & Sullivan 2001 are observational field studies (greenery raised social use / reduced aggression in public housing) — correlational-leaning, not RCT; Alvarsson et al. 2010 (nature sound aids stress recovery) and Kaplan 1995 (ART) are real. The greenery→social-use anchor is the load-bearing one and it is genuine. Present as observational, not causal.
C30.reasons: Genuine AND-gated geometric-mean compound with a real greenery→social-use anchor; needs material segmentation of the dominant seating plane and abstains when UNKNOWN → AMBER. A-vs-B (planted upholstered glare-controlled corner vs open hard-floored overhead-lit desk grid) follows from the gate. Viable at AMBER provided the soft-material/absorption double-count is resolved and the task-conditional sign-flip (softness reduces alerting for deep solo focus) is retained.

C31.is_it_really_compound: Yes. A three-way content-gated product where any zero factor collapses the score: a deep view of a parking lot (veg_in_view~0) and a nature view with an exposed back (refuge~0) both drive it to zero. The value comes from the JOINT satisfaction of prospect AND nature-content AND protected-back, not from any average. The content-gating of vegetation to the DISTAL view cone (not the whole frame) is what lifts it above a plain prospect+refuge sum — a lush foreground with a blank distal wall scores low. Genuine interaction.
C31.citation_concern: Anchors are real and, crucially, the effect-anchor matches the claimed sign: Ulrich 1984 and Ulrich et al. 1991 (SRT) are genuine STRESS-RECOVERY / physiological results (not mere preference), so 'improves restoration/lowers arousal' is properly grounded. Appleton 1975 and Dosen & Ostwald 2016 legitimately support the prospect-refuge leg. No overclaim.
C31.double_counting_risk: Low-to-moderate, and self-limited: the base_measures list names both 'prospect' AND 'C11 prospect-refuge', which would double-weight prospect — but the computation actually multiplies prospect x veg x REFUGE (the enclosure-behind component of C11 only), so if implementation truly takes only the refuge sub-signal there is no double count. Must be enforced: do NOT multiply full-composite C11 (which already contains prospect) by standalone prospect. Also note prospect and refuge both feed the prospect-refuge hedonic already; recomputing here re-weights that axis — acceptable because the veg-gated recovery construct is distinct, but flag it.
C31.fixed_computation: recovery = prospect * veg_in_view * refuge_only, where refuge_only is the protected-back/enclosure sub-component, NOT full C11. ABSTAIN without a Tier-B plan or a detected seat plane, and abstain when the distal-view depth segmentation confidence is low (veg-in-cone rides segmentation → AMBER).
C31.reasons: Cleanest candidate in the set alongside C34: real multiplicative content-gate, computable at Tier A+B, and the strongest literature (Ulrich is an actual recovery/physiology finding, matching the claimed sign). Occupant-state-specificity (helps a lone waiter, not a huddle) is stated honestly and the A-vs-B prediction (window-side wall-backed chair facing planting vs backless mid-floor bench at a blank wall) follows directly from the computation. Ceiling correctly AMBER because veg-in-view rides heuristic segmentation and seat-plane inference. VIABLE provided the refuge-only extraction is enforced so prospect is not double-weighted.

C32.is_it_really_compound: Yes at the top level: veg_frac x bandpass(fractal) x clutter-suppressor. The bandpass is a genuine non-monotone inverted-U (peak ~1.4, penalising both low and high fractal), and clutter enters as a multiplicative suppressor, not an additive term — mid-band natural texture AND low clutter are required simultaneously. That is a real interaction. HOWEVER the decisive risk is that fractal dimension and the clutter/processing-load proxy may be near-collinear (both are texture-complexity measures); if clutter is essentially high edge-density/high-fractal, then bandpass(fractal) x (1-clutter) is one complexity axis fighting itself, and the true compound degenerates to veg x complexity-band — still compound (2 real measures) but not the 3 claimed. Must verify fractal⊥clutter empirically before trusting the third term.
C32.citation_concern: Kaplan & Kaplan 1989 / Kaplan 1995 (soft fascination, ART) are real and on-point. But Hagerhall et al. 2004 and Taylor 2006 are PREFERENCE/aesthetic-response studies, here recruited partly for a RESTORATION/dwell claim; Joye & van den Berg 2011 is honestly flagged as contesting the biophilic-restoration story. The proposer concedes 'restoration link partly contested', which is the correct honest posture. Separately, the SOCIAL leg ('lingering seeds chance social contact') rests on none of the cited studies — ART/fractal work is not social-interaction research; that inference borrows Whyte-style co-presence logic not present in the citations. Downgrade the social claim to inferential.
C32.double_counting_risk: Two flags. (a) Fractal dimension already feeds the hedonics/perceptual-fluency inverted-U layer (Reber/Winkielman); reusing it here re-weights the same fluency signal. (b) Possible fractal↔clutter collinearity (see compound test) risks silently double-representing complexity within the attribute itself.
C32.reasons: Genuine non-additive structure and fully Tier-A computable, so it survives — but it is a vegetation-flavoured re-use of the fractal-fascination interaction with three soft spots: contested restoration evidence, a social claim not supported by any cited social study, and a real collinearity threat between its fractal and clutter terms. Keep only if (i) fractal⊥clutter is checked so the third factor is not decorative, and (ii) the social/dwell claim is demoted to an uncertainty-flagged inference. AMBER is correct (veg rides segmentation, restoration link proxy-grade).

C33.is_it_really_compound: Yes as a spatial co-occurrence product: veg_frac x integration x seat_present, where integration~0 off the movement network zeroes isolated greenery. The signal is the JOINT location of nature + a path-crossing node + a reason to stop, which no single primitive carries — a planter in a dead-end and an integrated crossing with no greenery both score low. Real interaction, not an average.
C33.citation_concern: Strong and, unlike C32, the social claim rests on SOCIAL studies: Coley, Kuo & Sullivan 1997 and Sullivan, Kuo & DePooter 2004 directly show greenery increases social use of common space; Whyte 1980 (triangulation) and Hillier & Hanson 1984 (integration) are real and apt. Evidence is the candidate's strongest asset.
C33.double_counting_risk: Significant. The base measure is stated as 'C1 visual integration / C23 commons gravity' — but C23 is ALREADY a commons-gravity composite (integration + social pull) that feeds the score layer. Multiplying veg by C23 double-counts the gathering-node property; the attribute then reduces to 'C23 with a green tint'. To stay honest it must multiply veg by RAW C1 integration (a primitive), not by the C23 composite. The genuinely new signal is the veg×integration gate (Coley/Kuo), which is defensible; the C23 route is not.
C33.fixed_computation: gravity = veg_frac * C1_integration_raw * seat_present (use C1, NOT C23). ABSTAIN without a Tier-B plan or declared integration. NOTE a real computability gap: 'seat_present' from a single photo needs object detection beyond classical CV — supply it from the Tier-B plan or a declared model, else abstain.
C33.reasons: Best-evidenced social attribute of the set and a real placement-conditional product, so VIABLE — but tightly bounded: it must consume raw C1 integration rather than the C23 commons-gravity composite (otherwise it double-counts and becomes a duplicate of C23/triangulation), and seat presence must come from plan/declared model, not photo semantics. Tier B, AMBER (integration rides inferred plan; veg rides segmentation).

C34.is_it_really_compound: Yes — the strongest non-additivity in the set. (daylight x absorption) x (1 - 2*relu(glare-g0)) has a SIGN FLIP: below the glare threshold the terms multiply (synergy, larger than either alone); above it the gate goes negative (antagonism, glare-dominated avoidance). No sum or average can represent a sign change, so this is unambiguously compound and directly encodes the brief's flagship synergy+antagonism pattern.
C34.citation_concern: Component anchors are real and on-target: Boubekri 2014 and Beute & de Kort 2014 (daylight→mood/vitality), Sarampalis 2009 (acoustic/listening-effort cognitive cost), Ulrich 1991. One honest caveat: every cited study is SINGLE-FACTOR; none measures the daylight×absorption INTERACTION, so the super-additive 'larger than either alone' magnitude is a composition hypothesis, not a measured effect. The antagonistic glare gate, by contrast, is well grounded (glare→discomfort/avoidance is settled). Present the synergy magnitude as a modelled prediction, not an established interaction effect.
C34.double_counting_risk: Moderate. Glare feeds the discomfort hedonic, daylight feeds C10/C22 circadian, absorption feeds C7/C20 acoustics — all three are re-weighted here. Acceptable because the sign-flipping interaction is genuinely novel (not captured by any single downstream layer) and the three inputs are relatively independent (low mutual collinearity, unlike C32/C36), so the compound is not silently re-representing one axis.
C34.reasons: Co-strongest with C31. Genuine sign-flipping product, all three inputs Tier-A computable (vertical-illuminance proxy, material-table absorption proxy, glare risk), and it operationalises exactly the daylight-with-acoustic-treatment synergy AND the untreated-glass antagonism the brief calls flagship. A-vs-B (shaded diffuse-daylit absorptive corner = positive talk pocket vs unshaded south glass + hard surfaces = negative) follows mechanically. VIABLE; AMBER because all three are proxy-grade and the synergy magnitude is a modelled composition rather than a measured interaction.

C35.is_it_really_compound: Partly. The TOP level is a real non-additive interaction: warmth_softness x bell(enclosure, peak~0.55) — the inverted-U bell means warmth in a too-open hall (reads institutional) or a too-enclosed cell (reads cramped) both score low, so warmth and enclosure genuinely gate each other; that survives the test. BUT the warmth_softness term is itself an explicit WEIGHTED AVERAGE (0.5*warm + 0.3*wood + 0.2*homogeneity) of three warmth-family measures — the disguised-average trap the brief warns about. So the attribute is really a 2-term compound (warmth-composite × enclosure-bell), not the 4-way interaction the base list implies. Honest label: 2 interacting constructs, one of which is a pooled average.
C35.citation_concern: Coburn et al. 2020 legitimately identifies 'hominess' as a distinct dimension of architectural experience (psychometric/neuro), and Chatterjee & Vartanian 2014 and Kaplan & Kaplan 1989 are real — but Coburn's hominess is an AFFECTIVE/perceptual dimension, described by the proposer as 'emerging'. The claim that hominess raises willingness to hold SUSTAINED/CONFIDENTIAL exchange is an inference beyond Coburn (no cited study measures conversation duration or disclosure), i.e. a social-interaction claim resting on a non-social affective study. Flag as inferential.
C35.double_counting_risk: Real. warm/cool ratio and wood coverage are correlated (wood is warm-toned), so averaging them inside warmth_softness partially double-counts warmth; and 'warm' already feeds the affect/hedonic layer. The pooled warmth term thus over-represents one signal.
C35.fixed_computation: Keep the genuine interaction: hominess = warmth_softness * bell(enclosure). To avoid the internal double-count, either drop wood OR down-weight it given its collinearity with warm, and treat warmth_softness honestly as a composite index rather than three independent measures. ABSTAIN when material confidence is low (wood vs laminate) — correctly noted.
C35.reasons: Survives because the enclosure-gated-by-warmth inverted-U is a true non-additive interaction with a plausible A-vs-B (wood-lined carpeted banquette alcove = settle and talk vs glass-walled atrium bench = pleasant but exposed), but it is weaker than C31/C34: the warmth leg is an average (mild double-count of warm/wood), the hominess→confidential-exchange link is inferential beyond the cited affective study, and wood/laminate material ambiguity caps it. VIABLE at AMBER only with the warmth-average double-count acknowledged and the social claim demoted to inference.

C36.is_it_really_compound: Core yes, but weakened by amplifier collinearity. The heart is angularity x specularity (threat co-occurrence: sharp AND glossy worse than either — a matte angular form or a glossy curved form scores far lower), which is a genuine product. The two remaining factors are multiplicative BOOSTERS, and both are near-collinear with a factor they boost: edge clarity amplifies angularity but edge sharpness and contour angularity are largely the same edge signal, and glare amplifies specularity but glossy surfaces PRODUCE glare — so (angularity·edge) and (specularity·glare) each partly let one measure amplify itself. The real compound is the 2-way angularity×specularity product; the edge and glare terms risk being self-amplification, not independent evidence.
C36.citation_concern: Bar & Neta 2006/2007 is a real AFFECT/threat result (angular contours judged threatening, amygdala response) and properly supports the angular leg with the correct sign. Vartanian et al. 2013 is real but a curvature-PREFERENCE/beauty study used for approach/avoidance — defensible but note the preference-vs-stress slippage. The specularity→threat leg is the weak one and the proposer self-flags Coburn 2020 as 'inferential (uncertainty flag)'; no biosignal study is cited for specular surfaces, so the 'loads physiology while reading premium' prestige-mask claim is an unevidenced physiological assertion on the specular half. Keep the specular leg uncertainty-flagged.
C36.double_counting_risk: Two collinear amplifier pairs: angularity↔edge clarity (shared edge-sharpness), and specularity↔glare (gloss generates glare). Both boosters double-count the primitive they multiply, inflating scores for scenes that are merely sharp-and-shiny along one axis. Also edge clarity and glare feed other layers already.
C36.fixed_computation: Reduce to the defensible core: avoidance = angularity * specularity, and only ADD edge/glare boosters if they are shown to carry variance independent of angularity/specularity respectively (otherwise they are self-amplification). Cap at AMBER and carry the specularity-threat uncertainty flag until a SUBJECTIVE→BIOSIGNAL→PHYSICAL triplet validates the specular→vigilance link.
C36.reasons: Retained as the honest negative pole of approach (Bar & Neta genuinely grounds angular→threat, and the A-vs-B — sharp polished-stone-and-mirror edge people traverse vs soft matte curved cluster people settle in — follows), but it is the shakiest of the six: two of its four inputs are collinear amplifiers that double-count, and the specularity-threat half is inferential by the proposer's own admission (a preference/premium reading sold with a physiological-loading claim no citation supports). VIABLE only trimmed to the angularity×specularity core with the specular leg uncertainty-flagged; AMBER.

C37.is_it_really_compound: Yes — genuinely compound. The value comes from an interaction, not a sum: a spatial gradient term (delta_field = far minus near across the threshold) MODULATED by an inverted-U gate on preview. Neither the gradient alone (high approach with zero preview = balk) nor preview alone predicts invitation; the product is the signal. It also legitimately compounds over another compound (approach_propensity_field), which is allowed.
C37.double_counting_risk: Real and specific: 'mystery occlusivity' is listed as a base measure AND the inverted-U on 'decision-point preview' (peaks at partial occlusion) re-encodes exactly the same occlusion-of-what-lies-beyond signal. Mystery ~= partial preview. As written the occlusion channel is weighted twice. Fix: drop the standalone mystery term and let the preview inverted-U carry mystery, OR use mystery for the gate and preview only for the tiebreak — not both as independent multiplicands.
C37.citation_concern: Kaplan & Kaplan 1989 and Vartanian 2015 are real but are PREFERENCE/aesthetic-rating findings; here they are extended to ENTRY/approach BEHAVIOR ('people balk', 'route onto desire lines'). Defensible extrapolation but it is a behavioral claim resting on preference studies — flag, do not treat as settled behavioral evidence.
C37.fixed_computation: Collapse the occlusion double-count (mystery XOR the preview inverted-U). Keep abstention when the onward region is not imaged/inferable.
C37.reasons: Compound is real (gradient x mystery-inverted-U). Two feasibility caps justify AMBER honestly: (a) approach_propensity_field and decision-point preview/mystery occlusivity are among the 50 UNCOMPUTED newly-proposed primitives, so it rides proxy-grade inputs; (b) it needs two region samples across a threshold, and abstains if the beyond-region is not imaged. The A-vs-B claim (curved brightening half-reveal vs blank/full-glazed) follows from the computation. Survives as VIABLE only after the mystery/preview double-count is fixed; keep at AMBER.

C38.is_it_really_compound: Yes — a proper conditional co-occurrence / AND-gated divergence detector. The signal is precisely the DISAGREEMENT (high conscious fluency co-occurring with high spectral discomfort); either alone scores zero. This is the textbook masking pattern and the interaction, not an average, carries the value.
C38.double_counting_risk: STRONGEST concern of all survivors. The fluency proxy explicitly includes a 'fractal-fit' (1/f) term, and 'spectral discomfort deviation' IS deviation from the 1/f amplitude spectrum. On that shared component the two 'independent' channels are near-inverse BY CONSTRUCTION (good fractal-fit = close to 1/f = low spectral deviation), so the AND-gate can rarely fire on the fractal axis and the divergence is partly self-contradictory. The genuine independent variance comes only from fluency's symmetry + edge-clarity terms vs. the Wilkins mid-band excess-energy signal. Fix: remove the fractal-fit term from the fluency proxy for this compound, so the two inputs are structurally independent.
C38.citation_concern: Anchors are real and on-point: Fernandez & Wilkins 2008 (uncomfortable images deviate from 1/f), Wilkins 1995, O'Hare & Hibbard 2011 (discomfort from filtered noise), Reber et al. 2004 (fluency->affect). Strong visual-stress evidence; no overclaim. Note the discomfort literature is asthenopia/headache in lab viewing — extrapolation to in-situ dwell erosion is proxy-grade, which is why AMBER not GREEN.
C38.fixed_computation: Redefine fluency without the 1/f/fractal term so it is structurally independent of the spectral-deviation input; keep the AND-gate on medians.
C38.reasons: Valid masking compound over Tier-A GREEN primitives, strong evidence, honest (no social form claimed). Held to VIABLE_CEILING_AMBER because (a) the fractal-fit/1-f overlap must be removed or the divergence is partly an artifact, and (b) the in-situ construct link (this divergence predicts real visual-stress/dwell loss) is proxy-grade. Lower social value since it is only two primitives with no A-vs-B, but honest and useful as a masking flag.

C39.is_it_really_compound: Yes in form — product-of-antagonists (coziness_affect x melanopic_deficit) across a nonlinear entrainment knee, with the deficit a threshold function. The co-occurrence of high hedonic warmth with sub-knee melanopic level is a real interaction, not an average.
C39.double_counting_risk: Present: C22 (circadian contrast) is a plan metric almost certainly already derived from warm/cool ratio + brightness/melanopic estimates, yet warm/cool ratio AND vertical illuminance proxy AND C22 all appear as separate inputs. C22 straddles both the 'coziness' and 'deficit' sides, double-weighting the same CCT/brightness signal. Separate the affect side (warm/dim) cleanly from the melanopic side and do not reuse C22 on both.
C39.citation_concern: Evidence is genuinely strong and correctly used: Brown et al. 2022 (>=250 melanopic EDI daytime), Lucas et al. 2014 (metrology), Chang et al. 2015 (evening light phase delay). No overclaim; the timing conditionality is honest.
C39.fixed_computation: Requires (1) time_of_day to resolve sign and (2) an absolute melanopic/illuminance reading, because a standard auto-exposed photo destroys absolute luminance — vEDI_proxy cannot be located relative to a 250-lux knee from pixels alone. Abstains on both counts as written.
C39.reasons: The core defect for computability is decisive: melanopic-EDI-from-a-single-photo is not recoverable (camera auto-exposure normalizes away absolute levels), so the knee threshold that makes the deficit meaningful cannot be measured without a sensor, and the sign cannot be set without time_of_day. The proposal itself marks tier 'extra_input' and abstains — that is the honest classification. Genuinely compound and well-evidenced, but not computable from the image alone: NEEDS_EXTRA_INPUT (time_of_day + melanopic reading), and fix the C22 double-count before wiring.

C40.is_it_really_compound: Yes in form — a product with an enclosure gate (nature_halo x stagnation_proxy) that fires only where a restorative cue co-occurs with high enclosure. The gate makes it an interaction, not a sum of nature cues.
C40.double_counting_risk: Explicit and severe on the halo side: score uses normalized(C19 + veg + wood), but C19 (restoration/nature contact) is itself built FROM vegetation fraction and natural-material/wood coverage. Summing a composite with its own ingredients literally double- and triple-counts vegetation and wood. This must be fixed (use C19 alone, or veg+wood alone — not the composite plus its parts).
C40.citation_concern: Evidence is strong and honestly deployed: Allen et al. 2016 (COGfx, cognition falls as CO2 rises), Satish et al. 2012, and critically Cummings & Waring 2020, which cleanly kills the 'plants clean the air' halo (potted plants remove negligible CO2/VOC). No overclaim.
C40.fixed_computation: The stagnation_proxy = 'enclosure with no visible operable aperture' is a WEAK ventilation proxy: an enclosed room may be mechanically (HVAC) ventilated and fresh, while an open/glassy room may be stuffy — enclosure does not entail low fresh-air exchange. And the physiological magnitude (CO2 cognitive decrement) is not imageable and is marked extra_input.
C40.reasons: The masking construct is valid and the Cummings-Waring anchor is exactly the right kill for the halo. But three problems hold it to NEEDS_EXTRA_INPUT: (1) the halo term double-counts veg/wood through C19 and must be rebuilt; (2) enclosure is a poor stand-in for ventilation, so the gate is confounded; (3) the physiological term abstains without a CO2 reading, which the image can never supply. Without CO2 it can only raise a soft risk flag, not measure the mask — so it needs extra input to become a real claim.

C41.is_it_really_compound: Yes — the cleanest interaction in the set. Two independent privacy CHANNELS (visual shelter, acoustic privacy) pulled apart by an antagonistic ratio; a nook high on both or low on both scores ~0, and only the DIVERGENCE (visually enclosed but acoustically leaky) scores. The value is the interaction, and it directly instantiates a masking/false-shelter pattern.
C41.double_counting_risk: Minor and manageable: visual_refuge = norm(enclosure + C11), but C11 (seat-level prospect-refuge) likely already incorporates enclosure, so enclosure is lightly double-weighted on the visual side. The acoustic side uses min(absorption, privacy_from_STI) rather than a sum, which mitigates the absorption->STI overlap. Recommend visual_refuge = C11 alone (which already carries enclosure) to remove the residual double-count.
C41.citation_concern: Anchors are real and appropriate: ISO 3382-3 (STI/privacy-distance methodology), Bradley & Gover 2004, Passero & Zannin 2012. The premise that visual enclosure and acoustic privacy are independent channels (a screened booth with hard surfaces leaks speech) is well established. No overclaim; this is an acoustics result used for an acoustics claim.
C41.fixed_computation: Drop enclosure from the visual sum (C11 already contains it). STI/speech-privacy needs a plan + occupancy/acoustic params; abstains when occupancy is UNKNOWN — honest.
C41.reasons: Highest-value survivor. It directly sharpens the confidential-exchange field David explicitly asked for, and the region A-vs-B claim (hard-surfaced screened alcove = false shelter that invites unprotectable talk vs. textile-lined corner = real privacy, route confidential exchange there) follows straight from the computation. Genuine ratio compound, strong evidence, honest tier. AMBER because it rides absorption proxy + inferred-plan/STI and abstains without occupancy. VIABLE after the small enclosure double-count is trimmed.

C42.is_it_really_compound: Compound in FORM only (product-of-antagonists visual_warmth x solar_thermal_exposure), but the interaction has no reliable signal to carry. By the proposer's own citations the hue-heat effect is 'small' (Fanger 1977) and 'small/inconsistent' (Toftum 2018). A product whose modulating construct is near-null is a formally-compound function that measures essentially noise.
C42.double_counting_risk: Secondary given the primary defect, but present: warm/cool ratio + colour palette entropy on the visual side, and glass coverage + vertical illuminance on the solar side, each pair shares a brightness/warmth signal, and warm/cool + vEDI recur across both terms.
C42.citation_concern: The citations are honest but self-defeating: they establish that the very effect the attribute exploits is small and inconsistent (contested link). Per the honesty constraints, the correct design for a contested link is an uncertainty flag or abstention — not a scored attribute. Tsushima 2020 is a perceptual-rating result, not thermal-physiology.
C42.fixed_computation: The real thermal cost (air temp/RH) is not imageable and is marked extra_input — it must be measured by a sensor regardless. Once you have that sensor reading, the visual-warmth 'mask' term adds little, because the physical thermal state is already known directly.
C42.reasons: Adversarially this does not clear the bar. (1) Its signal rests on an interaction its own evidence calls small and inconsistent — a masking detector whose 'mask' is a near-null perceptual effect is not detecting a robust mask. (2) The thermal cost it claims to expose can only be established with a temperature/RH sensor, which makes the visual term nearly decorative once that input exists. (3) No social form, no A-vs-B, lowest rank (4). KILL is the respectable call; at most demote to a low-confidence uncertainty flag, but it should not ship as a scored compound attribute.

C43.is_it_really_compound: Genuinely compound. The value is NOT the sum — the base=Σs_i term is discardable; the load-bearing signal is the pairwise PRODUCT synergy=beta*(s_g*s_r + s_g*s_t + s_r*s_t), which is zero when only one stressor is active and only fires on co-occurrence above each knee. That is a true threshold-gated interaction, exactly the 'super-additive' pattern the CNfA doc names. Passes.
C43.double_counting_risk: Mild. glare risk, absorption proxy and warm/cool ratio very likely already feed the hedonics/affect layer; re-multiplying them here re-weights the same three primitives. Acceptable because the SYNERGY term is new information (an interaction the primitives cannot express), but the additive base term should be down-weighted or dropped so it does not silently re-sum signals already scored elsewhere.
C43.citation_concern: Hygge & Knez 2001 (noise/heat/light × memory), Witterseh/Wyon/Clausen 2004 (noise×temp × office work), Schweiker et al. 2020 (combined IEQ) are all real and genuinely document non-additive interaction — good anchors. BUT the studies concern ACTUAL thermal load; this compound's third stressor is 'warm/cool ratio', a COLOUR-palette proxy, not temperature. Warm-toned finishes do not impose the thermal physiology those papers measured. The synergy structure and the glare×acoustic pair are well-supported; the thermal leg is a proxy substitution the cited literature does not license.
C43.fixed_computation: Keep the pairwise-product synergy as the reported value; either drop the additive base term or flag it as non-compound. Replace/label the thermal leg: warm/cool ratio is a colour proxy, so the thermal-stress interpretation ABSTAINS without a declared temp/RH; glare×reverberation super-addition stands on its own as the defensible core.
C43.reasons: Real interaction (product, not average), Tier-A computable, non-additivity well-evidenced for glare×acoustic. Ceiling AMBER because absorption proxy rides material-over-segmented-plane heuristics. Two live flags: (a) warm/cool colour ratio is not thermal load — the cited papers are about real heat, so the thermal leg overclaims; (b) mild double-count of primitives already in the hedonics layer via the additive term. Defensible to Kirsh once the thermal leg is honestly labelled a colour proxy and the additive term de-weighted.

C44.is_it_really_compound: Yes — buffered_load = L*(1 - lambda*a) is slope-changing, not averaging: agency alters the stimulus→stress gradient so the SAME physical load yields less residual load. That is the documented agency-buffer interaction (Glass & Singer). Genuine compound-over-compound.
C44.double_counting_risk: Low. C17 here modulates rather than adds; glare/absorption enter only through L. As long as L is the C43-family load and not re-scored independently downstream, no double weighting.
C44.citation_concern: Strong and correctly typed. Glass & Singer 1972 is causal and a STRESS (aftereffect) result, not a preference study — exactly right for an effect_sign claim about stress. Boerstra et al. 2016 and Leaman & Bordass 1999 ('perceived control a killer variable') are real and on point. No overclaim.
C44.fixed_computation: No math fix needed. Requirement: it must fire only when C17 (from a declared 3D/controls model) or a validated controls-detector is present, and ABSTAIN otherwise — which the candidate already specifies.
C44.reasons: Conceptually the strongest-evidenced item, and honestly self-abstaining. But 'visible means of control' (operable blind, dimmer, thermostat, movable chair) is not recoverable from a single photo, and C17 itself is not reliably derivable from vanishing-point plan inference — it needs a declared model or real controls audit. Because it cannot fire on Tier A / inferred-plan alone, the honest verdict is NEEDS_EXTRA_INPUT, not VIABLE. Keep it — it is the right correction — but gate it on the declared-control input.

C45.is_it_really_compound: The interaction FORM is real: index = kappa * reverberation * neutral_band(warmth) is a band-gated product, nonzero only when high reverberation co-occurs with thermal neutrality. Not an average. So it passes the compound test in principle.
C45.double_counting_risk: Secondary to the computability problem. material coverage (wood/stone) is listed as a base measure but does not appear in the computation (only w and r) — the input list is loose and should be reconciled.
C45.citation_concern: The PHENOMENON is real and correctly cited: Pellerin & Candas 2003/2004 (noise shifts thermal preference), Schweiker 2020, Torresin 2021 — genuine cross-modal misattribution evidence. Not overclaimed.
C45.fixed_computation: neutral_band must be gated on a MEASURED/DECLARED thermal state (air temp/RH in the neutral band), not on warm/cool colour ratio. As specified it ABSTAINS on Tier A and only computes when a physical thermal input is supplied; the material coverage input should be dropped or folded into a stated warmth model.
C45.reasons: Fatal-as-specified but salvageable, hence not a clean KILL. The gating variable is 'physical warmth sits in the neutral band', but the computation reads that from warm/cool COLOUR ratio — a category error (colour-temperature appearance ≠ thermal comfort, and colour is confounded by white balance, materials, lighting). A photo cannot know a room is thermally neutral, so labelling this Tier A and computing neutrality from colour is exactly the invented-mapping / fabrication the honesty rules forbid — it would fire on a cool-toned hot room and miss a warm-timber cold one. The idea (acoustic→perceived-warmth misattribution diagnostic) is worth keeping ONLY if it takes a declared thermal input and abstains without it; that makes it NEEDS_EXTRA_INPUT, and its self-assessment as Tier A must be rejected.

C46.is_it_really_compound: Yes, and the decisive test passes: initiation = sg*(1 - L). At L→1 the geometric conversation signal is driven to zero regardless of how favourable the seating/anchor/integration are — a genuine multiplicative suppression, not a blend of load and geometry. The corrective information lives entirely in the product.
C46.double_counting_risk: Mild and intentional. glare and absorption feed both the environmental-load family (C43) and this gate; that is legitimate compound-over-compound reuse, but if C43's load and this gate are both surfaced as scores, note that the same two primitives now influence two reported fields.
C46.citation_concern: Whyte 1980 (triangulation) and Osmond 1957/Sommer 1969 (sociopetal/sociofugal) are genuine SOCIAL-interaction findings — correctly social, not a social claim resting on a non-social study. Glass & Singer 1972 / Cohen 1980 support stress→reduced prosocial engagement. Anchors are honest.
C46.fixed_computation: No fix required; sg must ABSTAIN on the sociopetal/orientation term without a seat map (as specified), degrading to salience+integration.
C46.reasons: A genuinely corrective compound that guards against geometry-only over-prediction of conversation hotspots — the A-vs-B example (sg~0.8,L~0.7→0.24 vs L~0.2→0.64) follows directly from the computation. Ceiling AMBER because C1 (VGA) rides the inferred plan and the sociopetal term abstains from a photo. Defensible to Kirsh.

C47.is_it_really_compound: Yes — a four-way AND-gate where any single component below 0.6 zeroes the flag, value = product of the four breaches. This is the purest interaction of the set: it fires only on the specific co-occurrence (open AND hard AND leaky-speech AND high-traffic), which no single primitive and no average could isolate.
C47.double_counting_risk: Real flag. acoustic absorption enters TWICE — once as acoustic_exposure=(1-A) and again inside C7 STI_leak (STI/RT60 is itself a function of absorption). The product therefore roughly squares the absorption signal, over-weighting acoustics within a term that is supposed to require four independent breaches. Recommend using absorption in only one leg (prefer the STI leg, since that is the validated speech-privacy construct) or explicitly orthogonalising the two.
C47.citation_concern: Strong, correctly-typed workplace evidence: Kim & de Dear 2013 (speech-privacy loss = largest open-plan dissatisfier), Banbury & Berry 2005, Hongisto 2005, Osmond 1957. No overclaim; the masking framing (buzzy subjectively while loading the WRONG occupants' physiology) matches the doc's mask trap.
C47.fixed_computation: Remove the redundant acoustic leg: use openness*STI_leak*path_overlap with a single absorption-derived speech term, or keep (1-A) only where STI is unavailable. Retain the all-four-above-threshold AND-gate and ABSTAIN when plane-seg is low or STI is underivable.
C47.reasons: Best-structured interaction in the batch and a valuable masking diagnostic (measures the physiology of the occupants the buzzy zone disrupts, not the talkers). AMBER because C6/C7 ride inferred plan + acoustic params the photo lacks. One correction required: the absorption double-count inside (1-A) and STI must be resolved so the flag does not silently privilege acoustics over the other three breaches.

C48.is_it_really_compound: Yes — belonging = gate(E) * T * warmth * (1+pers): an enclosure-threshold gate that collapses the whole term below a defensible edge, times a personalization MULTIPLIER (not addend). Genuine gated product, not an average.
C48.double_counting_risk: Two flags. (a) enclosure appears both as the explicit gate AND inside C16 territoriality (defensible-space/territoriality is partly derived from enclosure), so enclosure is counted twice. (b) warm/cool ratio and material coverage both feed 'warmth' here and also feed C43 and the hedonics layer, so the warmth signal is re-weighted across multiple compounds. Neither is fatal but both should be disclosed / orthogonalised.
C48.citation_concern: Real and appropriately scoped to attachment/well-being (self-report), not oversold as physiology: Wells 2000 (personalization→well-being), Knight & Haslam 2010 (empowerment/greening→well-being & productivity), Altman 1975 (territoriality), Sundstrom 1986. Correlational/satisfaction evidence sold as a belonging/attachment claim — consistent, no overreach.
C48.fixed_computation: Keep the enclosure gate and personalization multiplier, but (a) source enclosure OR C16 in the term, not both; (b) require desk-personalization from a semantic detector or declared model and ABSTAIN otherwise, as specified.
C48.reasons: Genuinely compound and honestly self-abstaining, but the personalization multiplier — recognising personal artifacts (photos, mugs, plants) as territory markers — needs object-level semantics classical CV lacks, and C16 territoriality needs a Tier-B/declared model. It cannot fire on Tier A. Combined with the enclosure double-count, the honest verdict is NEEDS_EXTRA_INPUT: retain the idea, gate it on the personalization/territoriality inputs, and de-duplicate enclosure.
